title: Associate, Operations
description: The Opportunity As a member of the Dublin Operations team, you will support the end\-to\-end trade lifecycle for Nearwater’s MiFID Regulated European business, with a primary focus on Total Return Swaps (TRS) and equity\-linked financing. You will be a day\-to\-day operational point of contact for clients, counterparties, and internal stakeholders across Origination, Trading, Middle Office, Finance, and Technology, contributing to scalable processes and controls as the Dublin office continues to grow. What You’ll Do **Support the trade lifecycle** * Support the end\-to\-end operational lifecycle for TRS and equity\-linked products, including resets, dividends, corporate actions, financing legs, collateral, and unwinds * Develop working knowledge of TRS documentation (ISDA/CSA), margin and collateral workflows, and equity swap mechanics * Support end\-to\-end operational processes for Repo transactions and LSTA/LMA loans alongside the core TRS book * Investigate and resolve reconciliation items, ensuring completeness and accuracy of books and records * Escalate settlement fails, outstanding margin calls, and disputes in a timely and transparent manner * Execute the daily operations controls and the End of Day process **Contribute to best\-in\-class processes** * Contribute to process design, automation, and the build\-out of the Dublin operations function * Maintain a strong control mindset, continuously monitoring processes and escalating potential issues * Support compliance with industry regulations and standards in relation to derivative transactions **Partner across the firm and externally** * Act as a day\-to\-day operational point of contact for client queries, ensuring timely, accurate resolution and a high\-quality client experience * Support Origination and Trading on new product and new client onboarding, including operational readiness for TRS structures * Liaise directly with our Custodian and Prime Broker on day\-to\-day operational matters * Contribute to KPI / KRI reporting and governance and management review materials * Provide partnership with colleagues in our New York, Toronto and London offices What We’re Looking Fpr **Experience and expertise** * 3–5\+ years of experience in operations, middle\-office, or trade support, with meaningful time spent on TRS and Equity Swap products at a bank, prime broker, hedge fund, or similar firms * Working knowledge of the TRS lifecycle, resets, dividend pass\-throughs, corporate actions, financing accruals, collateral / IM / VM, and termination mechanics * Working knowledge of TRS and equity swaps; exposure to Repo and LSTA/LMA loan products * Familiarity with ISDA Master Agreements, CSAs, and confirmation processes for equity TRS * Exposure to process re\-engineering, control build, and partnering with Technology on operational tooling * Strong experience with reconciliation processes, trade lifecycle management, and exposure/margin workflows * Hands\-on experience with Cash and Equity Swaps, Loans Administration, and EMIR / MiFID reporting required * Experience working in a MiFID investment firm preferred * Working knowledge of financial markets and industry technology including AcadiaSoft, ClearPar, GLAS, and Bloomberg * Proactive, initiative\-taking individual who can effectively manage multiple priorities and operate through ambiguity * Ability to thrive in a structured, high\-volume, high\-risk, time\-sensitive, and demanding environment * Excellent verbal and written communication, with confidence in client and counterparty calls * Strong technical aptitude, including high proficiency with advanced Excel and the ability to adopt new systems and tools * Capable of collaborating effectively within a team and excelling when working independently What You’ll Get * Opportunity to help build the Dublin operations function, with direct exposure to firm leadership in New York and London * Joining a firm in the early stage of its evolution in Dublin, with meaningful impact on the company’s growth and customer success * A platform to grow your operational career as the Dublin office scales * Inclusive workplace that values diversity and ensures everyone has a voice * Collaborative, supportive culture where new ideas are encouraged and contributions are recognised * Exposure to complex, high\-impact work, giving you hands\-on experience across the wider Dublin teams (Risk, Finance, and Trading) that you simply don’t get in larger organisations * A fast\-moving, intellectually challenging environment where continuous learning is part of everyday life * A collaborative culture where we work hard, support each other, and enjoy what we do * A competitive compensation and benefits package designed to reward your contribution and growth How You Work * You take ownership and operate with a strong control mindset * You’re detail\-oriented and accurate, with a high bar for books and records * You’re proactive and initiative\-taking, comfortable managing multiple priorities and operating through ambiguity * You collaborate effectively within a team and excel when working independently * You communicate clearly with clients, counterparties, and internal stakeholders at all levels * You thrive in a structured, high\-volume, high\-risk, time\-sensitive, and demanding environment Who We Are Nearwater Capital is a rapidly growing and innovative specialty finance company headquartered in New York City with a global presence and client base. Established in 2017, Nearwater has distinguished itself as a leader in delivering thoughtful, solutions\-based financing to some of the largest financial institutions and financial services firms in the world. Nearwater is expanding its business in Europe and has established an office in Dublin. Nearwater Capital Markets Europe (NCME) is authorised by the Central Bank of Ireland (CBI) as a MiFID investment firm. Nearwater offers an attractive market compensation and benefits package. As a creative, intellectual capital firm, Nearwater is proud to be 100% work from office.

title: Basic Grade Social Worker - CDNT 97856
description: **Before you apply** -------------------- This job opportunity is open to both HSE and non\-HSE applicants.**Reference** ------------- 97856 **Category** ------------ Health and Social Care Professionals **Grade** --------- Social Worker 3017 **Advertisement source** ------------------------ Section 38 **Advertisement Type** ---------------------- Open to all **Important Information** ------------------------- The HSE is advertising this job on behalf of a publicly funded organisation. title: Business Analyst
description: We’re looking for a **Business Analyst** to join our Transformation team in Dublin. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering high\-quality analysis across regulatory and strategic initiatives, ensuring strong outcomes for our customers and the wider business. You’ll work across cross\-functional teams to understand business needs, map processes and support the delivery of solutions that are fit for purpose, compliant and aligned to our customer\-first principles. **Duties \& Responsibilities:** * Elicit, analyse and document business and regulatory requirements, ensuring clarity, traceability and alignment to business needs * Conduct process mapping, impact assessments and gap analysis to support decision\-making and solution development * Support solution design by articulating business needs, constraints and translating complex information into clear, structured and user\-friendly documentation * Facilitate workshops and collaborate closely with business, technology, risk, compliance and delivery stakeholders to align on scope, priorities and requirements * Support delivery across the full change lifecycle, including developing user stories and acceptance criteria, and supporting requirements validation, testing and implementation **Skills \& Experiences:** * Experience working as a Business Analyst or in a similar role within financial services, ideally supporting complex regulatory change initiatives * Strong knowledge of requirements elicitation, process mapping and impact analysis techniques, with the ability to apply them across complex environments * Proven ability to translate business needs into clear, structured requirements, user stories and supporting documentation * Experience working with stakeholders across business and technical teams, with strong influencing, communication and stakeholder management skills * Proficiency in tools such as JIRA, Excel and PowerPoint, with a proactive, organised approach to managing information and coordinating delivery outputs **What will you get for this role?** The work you do at Aviva will make a real difference to our customers. We will recognise your contributions and reward you. From competitive salaries to generous pension contributions and paid paternity leave; take a look through some of our employee benefits! The expected pay range for this role will fall between €60,000 and €80,000 and salary offered will commensurate with candidate's level of skills and experience. * A competitive market leading salary depending on skills, experience, and qualifications. * Generous pension (employee contribution matching of up to 14% if you contribute 8%). * Annual performance related bonus and pay review. * Holiday allowance of 29 days plus bank holidays, and the option to buy/sell up to 5 additional days. * Generous Health \& dental insurance contributions. * Aviva Matching Share Plan and Save As You Earn scheme and Approved Profit Share Scheme. * TaxSaver Travel Scheme. * Up to 40% discount for some Aviva products plus discounts for Friends and Family. * Professional qualification support and transparent career progression plans. * Brilliantly supportive policies including parental and carer’s leave. * Flexible benefits to suit you, including sustainability options such as cycle to work . * Make a difference, be part of our Aviva Communities and use your paid volunteering days to help others. * We take your wellbeing seriously with lots of support and tools. * We’re deeply invested in our people **, our total reward package** has so much to offer. **Aviva is for Everyone** Our diversity and inclusion policies and initiatives are shaping an environment where everyone feels welcome regardless of age, disability, race, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, religion, culture, sexual orientation, national origin, marital status, pregnancy, maternity or those with other caring responsibilities. Our approach helps to ensure that Aviva is a place which values difference and provides equal opportunities for all. We flex locations, hours and working patterns to suit our customers, business, and you. Most of our people are smart working – spending at least 50% of their time in our offices every week \- combining the benefits of flexibility, with time together with colleagues. We prefer all applications to be submitted online, however if you require an alternative method of applying please contact kate.ohara@aviva.com

title: Business Analyst
description: Job Title: Business Analyst – Payments, Fraud \& SCA Location: Dublin (Onsite 1 day per week) Contract Duration: Initial 6\-month contract (extendable) Start Date: ASAP Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with strong expertise in Payments, Fraud, and Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) to join the Banking Consulting Group. The successful candidate will play a key role across the full project lifecycle, including requirements gathering, process mapping, solution architecture and design, development support, UAT, and deployment. The ideal candidate will have 6–10 years of experience within the banking and financial services sector, with a strong background in large\-scale regulatory and payments transformation projects. This role requires close collaboration with client business and IT stakeholders, as well as internal development teams, to deliver high\-quality payment solutions. Key Responsibilities Engage with stakeholders to gather and analyse business requirements Map and document business processes and payment workflows Contribute to solution architecture and functional design discussions Support development, testing, UAT, and deployment activities Act as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in Payments, Fraud, and SCA Lead domain discussions and provide consultancy to BFSI clients Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure successful project delivery Required Skills \& Experience 8\+ years of experience in Payments domain Strong expertise in Fraud Prevention and SCA regulations/processes Proven experience in Business Analysis for Payments solutions Experience working on large regulatory or transformation programmes Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills Ability to lead functional and domain discussions with BFSI customers Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and documentation skills

title: Business Developer Project Intern (TikTok Shop) - 2026 Start (BS/MS)
description: **Responsibilities** We are looking for an ambitious Intern to support TikTok Commerce in Ireland. The role is part of the Account Management team which is pivotal to onboarding and ensuring the success of key partners on our platform, curating an engaging product selection for TikTok users. Together, we'll redefine how partners engage with customers through content and social commerce, creating a unique and innovative commerce ecosystem. As a project intern, you will have the opportunity to engage in impactful short\-term projects that provide you with a glimpse of professional real\-world experience. You will gain practical skills through on\-the\-job learning in a fast\-paced work environment and develop a deeper understanding of your career interests. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis \- we encourage you to apply early. **Responsibilities** * Assist in lead mining, qualifying, segmenting and other lead management works based on a data\-driven approach. * Work with the team to build showcases and case studies, including targeting, performance tracking and analysis. * Operate and Incubate TikTok Commerce via different channels such as external communities, emails, phone calls, online/offline events, helping with daily operation, related data consolidation and analysis to boost their growth. * Assist in market research and produce insights to feed the team targets and business strategies. * General administrative support to the operation team. * Other miscellaneous/administrative responsibilities as required by management. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications * Currently enrolled in a bachelor's or master's degree, graduating in 2026 * Strong written and verbal communication skills in English * Demonstrated learning ability, pro\-active and resilient, able to cope well under pressure, and pay attention to detail * A team player with excellent organizational \& planning skills * The ability to measure performance based on data analysis * Sound judgment and flexibility in balancing requirements, tight deadlines, and keeping people and projects moving on schedule Preferred Qualifications * Demonstrated computer skills and familiarity with MS office applications. Data processing using SQL or tableau is a plus. * Familiar with the E\-commerce market and social media trends Job Information **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. Why Join Us Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. Diversity \& Inclusion TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: Business Development Representative
description: *If you are a current employee who is interested in applying to this position, please navigate to the internal* *Careers* *site to apply.* **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. We are excited to have you apply to join our MarketStar team and can’t wait to discuss how we can help you find growth! **About the Business Development Representative** MarketStar is looking for a sales professional to represent our client as they continue their successful expansion into the enterprise corporate space. This is an exceptional opportunity to be a strategic leader on a pivotal team at the forefront of the latest technology and work with one of the top three companies in the world. Your responsibility will be to generate and qualify sales‑qualified opportunities within complex enterprise and corporate accounts. You will be a key contributor to MarketStar's mission by developing and executing strategic outreach plans and acting as a subject matter expert on how our client’s solutions can transform business operations. You will cover the EMEA Enterprise and Corporate markets, but may also provide mentorship and strategic support across other regions. **Location:** Hybrid, Leopardstown , Dublin 18 (Tuesday, Wednesday \& Thursday in office) **What Will You Do?** * Proactively seek new business opportunities in the market * Contact potential clients through cold calls and emails with the power of the brand behind you * Explore new business opportunities within certain target industries and personas * Present product value to potential clients * Identify client needs and suggest appropriate solutions * Proactively seek new business opportunities in the market * Set up meetings or calls between (prospective) clients and enterprise sales team * Adhere to daily, weekly and monthly KPIs * Achieve established sales goals and objectives * Serve as product and service advocate for the client, build interest and awareness of client’s value proposition, and assess mutual fit * Learn and maintain a current, comprehensive knowledge of client’s products and services * Champion our success stories and share with targeted companies in interesting industries and territories. **What Will You Need to Succeed?** * Fluent in English (Read, Write \& Speak) * 2 years of BDR experience * Experience working with IT decision makers * Experience with outreach sales strategies and prospecting companies in the EMEA region * Highly driven with a passion for sales and for developing as a sales professional * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Track record of achieving sales quotas * Business Development or sales experience (SaaS experience preferable) * Strong sales aptitude skills, solutions oriented with a passion for the customer and the customer experience * CRM, Salesforce, SalesLoft, Outreach.IO and other sales tech stack experience preferred. * Tech savvy with a passion for technology and the ability to learn, comprehend , and explain technology. * Experience self\-prospecting leads and building your own pipeline **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Employer\-Matched Pension \& Education reimbursement (After 6 Months) * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is base salary per annum of €* *40,000* *with an* *additional* *variable bonus* *component* *of €* *20,000\.* *There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process* *.* *MarketStar is* *firmly committed* *to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable* *accommodations are* *available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual who identifies as someone with a disability and require reasonable accommodation(s) to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at* people.success@marketstar.com *for* *assistance* *.*

title: Business Support, Administrative Assistant
description: **JLL** Our people at JLL and JLL Technologies are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology for our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people and empowering them to thrive, grow meaningful careers and to find a place where they belong. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades or technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, join our team as we help shape a brighter way forward This is a part\-time role, offering flexibility and freedom in structuring working hours, whether through full days or half days, accommodating both business needs and personal work\-life balance preferences. **General** * Provide full administrative support to the team in the office. * Coordinate with the offshore PDS Business Support team in the JLL Business Services Function (JBS) for designated tasks and business processes.. Example tasks include but not limited to: * Booking travel and processing expenses Scheduling meetings, booking meeting rooms and coordinating refreshments as required. * Typing and formatting of technical reports and presentations. Utilising other remote options such as backup typing as necessary. * Document production using Microsoft Office 365 software including Nitro/Adobe. * Co\-ordination of new starters/leavers process in conjunction with PDS Operations team and local office protocols. * Set up project files and maintain accurate filing according in iManage * Maintain accurate equipment records and inventory management * Maintain client contact details in CRM system i.e. Salesforce * Deal with staff and client queries as required. **Communications** * Coordinate and distribute team communications, announcements, and policy updates * Actively facilitate communication flow between local office, national business support and operational teams * Manage team distribution lists and ensure accurate contact information * Maintain professional telephone and email etiquette as first point of contact * Organise and coordinate internal team meetings * Prepare meeting materials, agendas, and presentation packages in accordance with best practice QA and branding standards * Prepare and distribute meeting minutes and action item summaries as required using JLL GPT. * Serve as a communication bridge between technical teams, management, and support functions * Coordinate with other JLL departments (HR, IT, Finance) for team requirements. * Coordinate with the PDS Pursuits Team for timely marketing collateral updates (including CVs, Case Studies etc.) **Financial \& Business Intelligence** * Process Purchase Orders, Speedchart numbers and third\-party consultant costs as required * Maintain Salesforce opportunity data and update previous entries to ensure accuracy of pipeline and close once secured/lost. * Maintain Certinia project data to ensure accuracy of secured work i.e. correct project end date, billing milestones to include correct naming convention, delivery owner, market, budgets etc. * Assist with financial reporting and reconciliation i.e. recoverable expenses, third party costs, invoices requiring evidence for the purpose of revenue recognition etc. * Support the monthly forecast collation process using Salesforce and Certinia. * Support month\-end and quarter\-end closing activities * Monitor financial metrics and actively drive progression through to completion i.e. aged debts, accruals, items pending revenue recognition. * Actively drive the timely submission and approval of timecards * Maintain vendor relationships **What You Can Expect From Us** You’ll join an entrepreneurial, inclusive culture. One where we succeed together – across the desk and around the globe. Where like\-minded people work naturally together to achieve great things. Our rewards program reflects our commitment to helping you achieve your ambitions in career, recognition, wellbeing, benefits and pay. Join us to develop your strengths and enjoy a fulfilling career full of varied experiences. Keep those ambitions in sight and imagine where JLL can take you.

title: Buyer
description: **Buyer – Retail, Front of Shop \- Hybrid in Dublin 24 (3 days in office)** This is not a “sit behind a desk and analyse spreadsheets” buying role. We’re partnering with a **large, fast‑moving Irish retail group (300\+ stores)** that is reshaping how it buys, ranges and launches branded products across its estate. They’re now hiring a **commercial Buyer** to take **full ownership of a Front of Shop category,** from supplier negotiation right through to in‑store activation. * **You own your category** * **You negotiate directly with global brands** * **You control promotions, space, launches and margin** * **You are accountable for the commercial number** If you’re looking **to step up in your career as a Buyer** , or are **frustrated by too many layers and not enough autonomy** , this is a genuine career‑accelerating opportunity. **What will I be doing?** * Owning the **end‑to‑end commercial cycle** for assigned categories * Negotiating with established global suppliers * Driving **promotional cycles** , JBPs, margin and sell‑through * Securing **exclusive launches, GWP activity and first‑to‑market deals** * Working closely with wholesale and retail teams to leverage buying power * Managing planograms, space allocation and range performance * Representing your category at weekly trade and commercial meetings **What experience do I need to be eligible?** * **2\+ years’ experience** in buying or assistant buying * Experience working with **branded supplier‑led categories** (e.g. Beauty, FMCG, Confectionery, Health, Electrical, Power Tools, etc.) * Comfortable **owning a P\&L number** * Can work independently in a **lean, hands‑on environment** **This opportunity is a full\-time permanent role, based in Dublin 24, hybrid (3 days onsite).** **To hear more about this position or to discuss your suitability, apply today and we will be in touch with more information.** **By applying here you agree to RECRUITERS storing your CV in our secure applicant tracking system (ATS; Bullhorn). As your consultants, we are dedicated to helping you find a new job in your field of expertise and, equally, to keeping your personal information secure at all times. Your CV will not be sent to any third party without your consent.**

title: Buyer Planner I - Remote Ireland
description: Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Beckman Coulter Diagnostics, one of Danaher's 15\+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we're all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You'll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher's system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact— innovating at the speed of life. As a global leader in clinical diagnostics, Beckman Coulter Diagnostics has challenged convention to elevate the diagnostic laboratory's role in improving patient health for more than 90 years. Our diagnostic solutions are used in routine and complex clinical testing, and are used in hospitals, reference and research laboratories, and physician offices around the world. Every hour around the world, more than one million tests are run on Beckman Coulter Diagnostics systems, impacting 1\.2 billion patients and more than three million clinicians per year. From uncovering the next clinical breakthrough, to rapid and reliable sample analysis, to more rigorous decision making—we are enabling clinicians to deliver the best possible care to their patients with improved efficiency, clinical confidence, adaptive collaboration, and accelerated intelligence. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The **Buyer/Planner I (Finished Goods)** is responsible for ensuring the efficient supply of finished goods by managing purchasing, inventory levels, and vendor performance, directly impacting customer satisfaction and operational efficiency. This position reports to the **Manager Materials \& Planning (Finished Goods)** and is part of the **EMEAI Finished Goods Planning** located in **a remote work arrangement** and will be fully remote. **In This Role, You Will Have The Opportunity To** * Manage purchasing and inventory processes, including placing POs and updating Oracle. * Monitor demand to maintain inventory targets and optimize stock levels. * Ensure supply of finished goods across EU warehouses. * Track and expedite late shipments and respond to supply issues. * Reduce excess stock and support purchasing/NPI projects. **The Essential Requirements Of The Job Include** * Honors Bachelor's degree (Level 8\) in a relevant field with at least 1\-2 years of relevant experience * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work in a methodical and accurate manner * Experience working in an MRP/MPS environment It would be a plus if you also possess experience in: * APICS Certification * Ability to use Oracle Manufacturing Beckman Coulter Diagnostics, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it's a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info. At Beckman Coulter Diagnostics we believe in designing a better, more sustainable workforce. We recognize the benefits of flexible, remote working arrangements for eligible roles and are committed to providing enriching careers, no matter the work arrangement. This position is eligible for a remote work arrangement in which you can work remotely from your home. Additional information about this remote work arrangement will be provided by your interview team. Explore the flexibility and challenge that working for Beckman Coulter Diagnostics can provide. Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real\-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life. For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

title: CPTO - Chief Product Technology Officer
description: **Chief Product \& Technology Officer** Dublin · Executive · Full\-time · Hybrid · HealthTech / SaaS A well\-established Irish healthtech business with a market\-leading position, a proprietary system\- SaaS platform, and an ambitious growth agenda across Ireland and the UK. A senior executive role reporting to the Managing Director. You'll own product and technology end\-to\-end — strategy, roadmap, platform, and team — with a seat at the leadership table and real autonomy to shape the company's next chapter. Responsibilities 1. Set and own the product and technology strategy 2. Lead development of the cloud platform, billing engine, and mobile apps 3. Build and lead the engineering and product teams 4. Drive the roadmap from discovery to delivery 5. Integrate AI and automation to improve efficiency 6. Uphold GDPR compliance and platform security Essential 1. Over 10 years senior product or technology leadership in Fintech/Healthtech/MedTech. 2. Delivered products to market – customer focussed CX journey 3. Team building and engineering leadership 4. Continuous improvement – Process Improvements\- Operational efficiencies 5. Commercially minded with product led revenue growth 6. Strong IT background in LAMP, AI and digital solutions, and Azure ( scaling cloud SaaS platforms) 7. Regulated / compliance environments

title: Chief Executive Officer
description: **CEO \- Founder\-Backed AI Infrastructure Company** A founder\-backed AI infrastructure platform is seeking a hands\-on CEO to lead the next phase of growth. The company has already built and deployed a functioning production\-ready platform enabling secure, high\-performance AI inference with integrated GPU provisioning for developers, SMEs, and enterprise environments. The core technology is operational. The next phase is commercial scale. This is not a ceremonial leadership role. It is an operating role for someone who wants ownership, accountability, and the opportunity to build a serious AI infrastructure business from early traction into scale. The successful candidate will work directly with the Founder, Board, and CTO and will be responsible for: * Commercial growth * Enterprise customer acquisition * Market positioning and narrative * Go\-to\-market execution * Strategic partnerships * Capital raising and investor engagement **What You’ll Be Doing** * Turning a working platform into paying enterprise customers * Defining a compelling market narrative * Building repeatable GTM and commercial motion * Leading enterprise sales and strategic partnerships * Working closely with technical leadership on product\-market alignment * Driving pilots into long\-term contracts * Building a lean, high\-performance operating culture **What We’re Looking For** This role suits someone who: * Has owned commercial targets previously * Has experience within SaaS, infrastructure, AI, cloud, or developer tooling * Can simplify complex infrastructure products commercially * Is comfortable operating with speed and ambiguity * Wants to build rather than inherit * Understands enterprise and regulated customer environments **This Role Is Not For** * Purely strategic executives * Operators reliant on heavy corporate structure * Remote\-first leaders * Candidates seeking low\-friction environments * Individuals motivated primarily by title progression For further information on this job contact the VANRATH office for a confidential chat today. Follow VANRATH on LinkedIn for expert career advice, the latest jobs, industry news and much more.

title: Civil Engineering Technician
description: **Overview** ------------ AtkinsRéalis one of the world's leading providers of engineering professional consulting and support services with offices across Ireland. We are looking for Civil Engineering Technicians at junior and mid\-career levels to become part of our dynamic team at AtkinsRéalis. This is an exciting opportunity to work within a multidisciplinary office on a wide range of projects, including land development for industrial, commercial, and residential schemes, as well as Aviation infrastructure. **Your role** ------------- * Create and manage accurate 2D drawings and 3D models for civil infrastructure (roads, drainage, utilities) using AutoCAD \& Civil 3D. * Conduct hydraulic and SuDS design using Causeway Flow. * Collaborate across the team for design integration and quality control. * Assist with design review, production detailing, and fabrication drawings. * Mentor junior technicians, providing technical oversight and guidance. * Adhere to design standards. **About you** ------------- * Excellent interpersonal, verbal and communication skills. * Flexible approach with ability to be able to multi\-task and prioritise when necessary. * Enthusiastic, hardworking, a positive outlook and approachable manner. * Well\-motivated, willing to take responsibility and able to work as part of a team or alone when required. **Rewards \& benefits** ----------------------- Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at every stage of your life and your career. Enjoy competitive salaries and a brilliant range of health, wellbeing, financial and lifestyle benefits. Make the most of a myriad of opportunities for training and professional development to grow your skills and expertise. And combine our hybrid working culture, international remote working opportunities, and flexible holiday allowances to balance a great job and fulfilling personal life. Be rewarded. **About AtkinsRéalis** ---------------------- We're AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. We connect people, data and technology to transform the world's'infrastructure and energy systems. Together, with our industry partners and clients, and our global team of consultants, designers, engineers and project managers, we can change the world. We're committed to leading our clients across our various end markets to engineer a better future for our planet and its people. **Additional information** -------------------------- We are committed to creating a culture where everyone feels that they belong \- a place where we can all be ourselves, thrive and develop to be the best we can be. So, we offer a range of family friendly, inclusive employment policies, flexible working arrangements and employee resource groups to support all employees. As an Equal Opportunities Employer, we value applications from all backgrounds, cultures and ability.

title: Cleaner
description: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. Benefits: * Free Food on shift * Plus tips \- You'll receive tips/Tronc\* * 50% off food and drink when you’re not working * Wellbeing Support for you and your family * Referral Bonus of €450 for referring pizza chefs and €1150 for referring managers * Pension \& Life Assurance * Pay Rate is €12\.80 per hour for under 20s and €13\.50 for age 20 and over * Learning and Development Opportunities to invest in you You’ll be the heart of our team, making sure that everything in our Pizzeria is spotless and restocked. You’ll create the perfect atmosphere in which our customers can have enlivening experiences and enjoy our famous pizza. Just be yourself and we will bring the best out of you. **No Experience Required.** What you’ll be doing: Run the pot wash, clean crockery and glassware and restock Clean floors and bathrooms Working in a dynamic and energetic environment Who we are: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. We’ve been around since 1965, bringing pizza to the UK and have since grown to a multichannel, international brand. Our people are at the heart of our success and everything we do; we believe you should always be able to express yourself. Here you can be yourself. Inclusivity and respect are at the heart of our business and are valued by our teams, customers, and suppliers. PizzaExpress and Milano is a place where everyone is welcome, and everyone belongs. That’s why we assess each application on the aptitude to do the job and nothing else. title: Clinical Specialist Occupational Therapist: Active Recovery Team (ART)
description: **Role Title:** Clinical Specialist Occupational Therapist: Active Recovery Team (ART) **Job Reference Number:** 002359 **Closing Date:** Monday, 1st June 2026 **Purpose of the Role** : The Active Recovery Team (ART) represents a pioneering “Discharge to Recover and Assess” service from an Irish level 4 hospital. The clinical specialist occupational therapist will provide the clinical leadership, advanced clinical knowledge and expertise to deliver a “Discharge to Recover and Assess” service to suitable inpatients at St. James’s Hospital. This outreach team provides timely re\-ablement and rehabilitation (in person and virtually) in the home .St. James’s Hospital, working in close collaboration with community partners is developing an “out of hospital care system” to support the needs of our population. This system comprises a range of intermediate care pathways to ensure patients receive the right care at the right time and at the lowest level of complexity. The Active Recovery Team (ART), providing a “Discharge to Recover and Assess” model is a key component of the “out of hospital care system” The Active Recovery Team (ART) is a goal\-focussed therapy\-led service supporting people to continue their physical and functional recovery in their own home following an acute hospital admission. The ART team aims to reduce hospital length of stay for those who are medically optimised and provide short\-term rehabilitation and re\-ablement at home, including the use of digital technology. The ART team comprises a Clinical Specialist Occupational Therapist, Clinical Specialist Physiotherapist and a therapy assistant. Operating a “Discharge to Recover and Assess” model, the ART team aims to: * Provide patient\-centred rehabilitation and re\-ablement upon discharge from hospital * Assess patients’ longer term needs and instigate early onward referral as appropriate * Implement a re\-ablement approach to reduce over\-prescription of home care packages The Clinical Specialist Occupational Therapist will possess skills and expertise greater than that expected of a senior occupational therapist, in particular related to clinical leadership, service development, clinical audit, education and research. The successful candidate will fully understand the principles underpinning a “Discharge to Recover and Assess” model embracing positive risk and supporting patient choice. **Required:** **Statutory Registration, Professional Qualifications, Experience, etc Candidates for appointmen** t must: Be registered, or be eligible for registration, on the Occupational Therapists Register maintained by the Occupational Therapists Registration Board at CORU. And 1. Have five years’ full time (or equivalent) years post qualification clinical experience of which four years’ full time (or equivalent) must be consecutive in the required area of specialism relating to acute general medicine, older person’s care in an acute hospital setting and/or rehabilitation. And 1. Have the requisite knowledge and ability (including a high standard of suitability, management, leadership and professional ability) for the proper discharge of the duties of the office And 1. Provide proof of Statutory Registration on the Occupational Therapists Register maintained by the Occupational Therapists Registration Board a **t CORU before a contract of employment can be issued** **2\. Annual Registration** 1. On appointment, practitioners must maintain annual registration on the Occupational Therapists Register maintained by the Occupational Therapists Registration Board at CORU And 1. Practitioners must confirm annual registration with CORU to Human Resources, St James’s Hospital by way of the annual Patient Safety Assurance Certificate (PSAC) **And** 1. Demonstrates an advanced level of clinical knowledge and skills in the assessment and treatment within acute general medicine 2. Demonstrates advanced clinical knowledge and skills in assessment and treatment within rehabilitation medicine 3. Demonstrates a proven track record in complex discharge planning 4. Demonstrates significant achievement in standard setting, clinical audit and research relevant to this specialty. Desired 1. Post graduate education and courses relevant to the specialty area.

Port Health or other state authorities. · Competency in using warehouse management systems and MS Office applications (Excel, Word, Outlook). · Ability to work independently, show initiative, and title: Data Architect
description: **Job Title:** Data Architect **Job type:** Permanent **Job Location:** Dublin/Hybrid **Company Overview:** A global data and artificial intelligence ("AI") company that offers services and solutions to reinvent client business models, drive better outcomes, and unlock growth with speed. The company harnesses the power of data, AI, and deep industry knowledge to transform businesses, including the world's leading corporations in industries such as insurance, healthcare, banking and financial services, media, and retail, among others. Founded in 1999, the company operates with the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity, and respect. **The Role:** Experienced Data bricks **Data Architect** required to support a high\-impact data transformation initiative focused on automating reporting and dashboard processes. You will work with structured and unstructured data sources, designing and building scalable ingestion and transformation pipelines within a Databricks medallion architecture. The role will involve stakeholder engagement, defining data acquisition approaches, orchestrating bronze/silver/gold data pipelines and establishing best practices across governance, lineage, data quality, performance optimisation and release management. Strong hands\-on experience with Databricks, modern data architecture and large\-scale data processing environments is essential. **Key Responsibilities:** * **Data Architecture \& Medallion Design:** Design and evolve scalable data models within a **Databricks Lakehouse architecture** , structuring data across **bronze, silver and gold layers** to support reporting and dashboard automation. * **Data Pipeline Design \& Delivery:** Lead the design and implementation of **data ingestion and transformation pipelines** , working across structured and unstructured data sources to enable reliable, production\-grade data flows. * **Resident Client Engagement:** Act as a **resident Data Architect on client site** , working closely with stakeholders to understand requirements, shape data solutions, and ensure alignment to business outcomes. * **Requirements Gathering \& Translation:** Engage with business and technical stakeholders to **gather, refine and define data requirements** , translating them into scalable and maintainable data architecture designs. * **Solution Evolution \& Ownership:** Take ownership of the **ongoing evolution of the data platform** , continuously improving architecture, pipelines, and data structures in line with changing business needs. * **Team Leadership (Onshore \& Offshore):** Lead and guide **distributed delivery teams (onshore and offshore)** , ensuring consistent standards, clear direction, and successful execution of data initiatives. * **Data Governance, Lineage \& Quality:** Establish and enforce best practices across **data governance, lineage, metadata management, and data quality** , ensuring trust and usability of data assets. * **Performance \& Optimisation:** Drive improvements in **data processing performance, scalability, and cost optimisation** , particularly within Databricks and distributed processing environments. * **Standards \& Documentation:** Define and maintain **data architecture standards, models, and documentation** , ensuring consistency, reusability, and alignment across delivery teams. * **Delivery \& Release Support:** Support **deployment and release processes** , ensuring data solutions are robust, properly governed, and production ready. **Experience Required:** * **Education \& Experience:** Bachelor's or master's degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field, with **significant experience (typically 8\-10\+ years)** in data architecture, data warehousing, and large\-scale data platforms, including **hands\-on experience in Databricks\-led environments** . * **Data Modelling Expertise:** Strong experience in **enterprise data modelling** across conceptual, logical, and physical layers, ideally within **data Lakehouse architectures** (tools such as ERwin, ER/Studio, Power Designer or equivalent). * **Databricks \& Data Platform Expertise:** Proven hands\-on experience designing and supporting **Databricks\-based data platforms** , including medallion architecture, Spark\-based processing, and scalable data pipelines. * **Data Integration \& Processing:** Experience delivering **data ingestion and transformation solutions** (batch and streaming) using tools such as Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), or similar modern data integration frameworks. * **Cloud \& Distributed Data Environments:** Strong understanding of **cloud\-native data platforms** (Azure preferred), including experience working with distributed data processing and integration with enterprise data stores (e.g., SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Oracle, Synapse). * **Business Intelligence \& Reporting Enablement:** Familiarity with **reporting and visualisation tools** such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar, with an understanding of how data architecture supports analytics and dashboarding. * **Industry Experience (Desirable):** Experience within **Insurance, Financial Services, or regulated environments** is advantageous. If you are interested in this role or would like to discuss it further, please call Nidhi at \+353 1 645 5244 or email nidhi.tripathi@experis.ie.

title: Data Center Infrastructure Technology Expert, DCS EMEA
description: **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. **Diversity \& Inclusion** TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too. **Team Insight** The DataCenter Service / Datacenter Cloud System (DCS) team sits within TikTok's global technology structure and supports the company's fast growth by building and operating hyper\-scale datacenters, managing the life cycle of server fleet, providing cloud solutions, and developing various infrastructure services, making sure they are scalable and are reliable. **Role Insight** The Global Data Center Infrastructure Technology Expert is a regional field engineering authority supporting hyperscale data center operations across EMEA. The role acts as the technical SME for critical mechanical, electrical, and life safety systems within live production environments. It ensures safe, reliable, and highly available infrastructure through strong field engineering expertise, fault resolution, vendor governance, and operational readiness support. The position bridges construction, commissioning, and live operations, ensuring smooth transition into steady\-state operations and continuous improvement across hyperscale data center facilities. **Responsibilities** \- Act as regional SME for mechanical, electrical, and life safety systems across hyperscale data center environments. \- Provide field engineering support to operations teams to maintain high availability, safety, and operational stability. \- Review design and construction deliverables to ensure field operability, maintainability, and compliance with hyperscale standards. \- Lead fault investigations, root cause analysis, and implementation of permanent corrective actions for critical infrastructure issues. \- Support operational readiness activities, including commissioning handover and transition into live production environments. \- Perform technical audits, risk assessments, and lifecycle evaluations of critical infrastructure assets. \- Manage vendor performance to ensure safe, compliant, and high\-quality delivery of maintenance and infrastructure works. **Qualifications** *Minimum Qualifications* \- Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, HVAC, or Building Services Engineering. \- 4\+ years’ experience in data centers or other critical environments (field engineering, operations, or facilities). \- Strong hands\-on knowledge of UPS, generators, LV/HV distribution, cooling systems, fire/life safety, and BMS. \- Experience working in live production environments with responsibility for uptime and operational performance. \- Ability to interpret technical drawings, schematics, and engineering documentation. \- Experience supporting vendors, contractors, or field service teams in mission\-critical environments. \- Strong troubleshooting, incident response, and root cause analysis capability. *Preferred Qualifications* \- 5\+ years of experience in data centers or critical environments with increasing technical responsibility. \- Experience with commissioning processes (L1–L5\), integrated systems testing, and operational acceptance. \- Apprenticeship or technical trade background in electrical or mechanical engineering. \- Certifications such as CIBSE, ASHRAE, BSRIA, Uptime Institute, or equivalent. \- Prior Senior Authorised Person (SAP) or equivalent electrical safety authorization, preferably in hyperscale, cloud, or large\-scale colocation data centers. \- Strong understanding of construction\-to\-operations transition, RIBA stages, and field engineering assurance. \- Experience in asset lifecycle management, reliability engineering, or failure reduction programs.

title: Data Engineer
description: Bentley Systems **Data Engineer** **Location:** Ireland and UK (Office/Hybrid/Home\-based) **Position Summary** Are you ready to architect the data foundation that drives our dynamic, consumption\-based business model? As a pivotal member of the Consumption Operations team, you will design, build, and maintain the mission\-critical data infrastructure that powers our global services. Success in this role means engineering robust, highly optimized data pipelines that guarantee the absolute quality and reliability of our data. Your work will directly impact the company's bottom line by ensuring 100% accurate, timely usage billing, while also unlocking data\-driven insights that empower the entire organization. **Responsibilities** * Pipeline Engineering: Design, build, and optimize scalable ELT/ETL pipelines to ingest, process, and transform massive volumes of structured and unstructured data. * Data Integration: Architect and maintain seamless integrations across diverse internal and external data sources, including databases, APIs, and business application software. * Governance \& Security: Champion data integrity and accuracy. Implement rigorous security measures and ensure strict adherence to financial data regulations, including SOX compliance. * Infrastructure Management: Proactively monitor system performance, troubleshoot bottlenecks, and continuously optimize the data infrastructure for maximum scalability, speed, and cost\-efficiency. * Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with data scientists, business analysts, and leadership to capture requirements and deliver high\-quality, reliable, and curated datasets. * Continuous Innovation: Maintain comprehensive documentation for data models and pipelines. Stay ahead of industry trends and introduce cutting\-edge methodologies and tools to our stack. **Qualifications** * A bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience as a Data Engineer. * 3\+ years of hands\-on experience as a Data Engineer, with a proven, impactful track record in a Data Engineer or heavily related data architecture role. * High proficiency in writing and optimizing SQL and Python. * Deep, hands\-on experience with modern cloud data platforms and tooling, specifically Snowflake, dbt, Fivetran, and SQL Server. * Exceptional problem\-solving capabilities, meticulous attention to detail, and the ability to thrive both autonomously and within a highly collaborative remote team. **What We Offer** * A great Team and culture – please see our colleague video. * An exciting career as an integral part of a world\-leading software company providing solutions for architecture, engineering, and construction \- watch this short documentary about how we got our start. * An attractive salary and benefits package. * A commitment to inclusion, belonging, and colleague well\-being through global initiatives and resource groups. * A company committed to making a real difference by advancing the world’s infrastructure for a better quality of life, where your contributions help build a more sustainable, connected, and resilient world. Discover our latest user success stories for an insight into our global impact.  **About Bentley Systems** Around the world, infrastructure professionals rely on software from Bentley Systems to help them design, build, and operate better and more resilient infrastructure for transportation, water, energy, cities, and more. Founded in 1984 by engineers for engineers, Bentley is the partner of choice for engineering firms and owner\-operators worldwide, with software that spans engineering disciplines, industry sectors, and all phases of the infrastructure lifecycle. Through our digital twin solutions, we help infrastructure professionals unlock the value of their data to transform project delivery and asset performance. www.bentley.com **Equal Opportunity Employer** Bentley is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and considers for employment all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, gender/gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, marital status, religion/belief, national origin, caste, age, or any other characteristic protected by local law or unrelated to job qualifications.

title: Data Privacy Specialist - 6 Month FTC
description: Created in 2003, Smartbox is the French and European number 1 in experience gifts, with 6\.5 million boxes sold each year and around €350 million annual turnover. For more than 20 years, we have been developing thousands of experiences and an equal number of unforgettable moments to live — from the most iconic to the most unique, covering stays, adventures, well\-being and gastronomy. Smartbox’s mission has always been to enable everyone to gift their loved ones the opportunity to live unique experiences. In the short future, we want to be the point of reference when it comes to inspiring people to live experiences. We are at a tipping point in terms of innovation on our market. The group employs 800 people across Europe. Smartbox gift boxes are sold in 10 European countries: France, Italy, Spain, Belgium, Switzerland, the Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden, Portugal and Germany. Pioneer in the sector and number 1 in most of these countries, we rely on a network of approximately 150 partner retailers throughout Europe — 18,000 major points of sale — and distribute through the websites of our various brands and online distributors such as Amazon. One of the keys to our success has been building a network of 30,000 partners in Europe. The Group has been owned since 2013 by Otium Capital, which has supported more than 50 start\-ups and innovative companies. More than just a box, Smartbox has been one of the biggest success stories in the industry over the last 20 years. **The Team:** The Smartbox Group Legal and Data Protection teams are small but high\-performing groups based in Dublin and Paris. The Data Protection team manages privacy and data protection matters for all countries across Europe, providing trusted advice to keep Smartbox’s partners’, customers’ and employees’ data safe. We are dedicated to building diverse and open teams where employees are valued for their contributions, perspectives and opinions. **The Role:** This Privacy Specialist role will report to Smartbox’s Senior Legal Counsel and Data Protection Department Manager based in Dublin. The successful candidate will: * Collaborate with our Legal and Data Protection teams in identifying and driving the appropriate strategies and procedures to ensure Smartbox’s regulatory obligations across Europe are met * Translate complex privacy and AI laws into practical advice for Smartbox’s Acquisition, Contracting, Marketing, CRM, Content and Employment teams as they shape the growth of our online platforms and employee experience * Drive initiatives across the organisation as we develop a world\-class privacy programme The position is based in Dublin, Ireland. The company operates a hybrid model with a mix of home and office working. **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide frontline privacy and data protection support to business stakeholders throughout Smartbox * Partner with business stakeholders, understand business needs, analyse privacy and data protection regulations, determine requirements, identify risks and implement business\-minded solutions * Ensure data privacy risks are considered at the outset of new projects; lead privacy impact assessments, evaluate risks and propose mitigating controls * Update records of processing, maintain data mapping and data inventory * Be responsible for the fulfilment of data subject rights requests * Support the investigation, resolution and reporting of data privacy complaints and incidents * Support periodic audits of the effectiveness of controls and risk capture mechanisms * Support the development of privacy\-related policies, notices and guidance * Support the development and implementation of scalable privacy and data protection processes and initiatives, including data retention policies, privacy impact assessments, data inventories, vendor controls and data subject requests * Develop and deliver privacy and data protection training and education initiatives * Identify opportunities to automate compliance activities and develop AI\-assisted workflows to improve efficiency * Keep informed on global privacy and data protection trends, legislation, and Data Protection Supervisory Authority decisions and guidance **Requirements \& Experience** **Qualifications \& Knowledge:** * Post\-secondary education with a focus on legal, risk, compliance or public policy matters * Knowledge of privacy laws and principles including the GDPR and e\-Privacy; IAPP or other privacy certifications an advantage **Experience:** * 1–3 years’ experience in a privacy, legal or compliance role (all levels of experience will be considered), such as privacy operations, online operations, vendor risk management or legal support * Familiarity and comfort with Data Subject Rights * Experience working with vendors and conducting privacy impact assessments * Familiarity with data transfer protocols and knowledge of the ways apps interact with and store data * Experience working with teams across multiple jurisdictions and cultures * Fluent spoken and written English; additional European languages an advantage **Skills \& Attributes** **Skills:** * Strong communication skills with the ability to influence effectively across a diverse range of stakeholders, negotiate, collaborate and effect change at all levels * Ability to understand a technology\-driven business * Ability to work independently and at pace, willing to share perspectives and support colleagues * Outstanding organisational and time management skills; ability to manage competing priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Flexible, open\-minded, adaptable, creative and solution\-driven **What We Look For:** We value ‘solution\-seekers’ rather than ‘problem spotters’ — people with an enthusiastic, positive and proactive attitude who believe we can always find ways to do things better. We love a good sense of humour and vibrant energy. You will also: * Have exceptional communication and organisational skills with the ability to multitask and deliver to deadlines * Be creative and innovative — able to spot new trends and develop an action plan * Balance attention to detail with swift execution * Be inquisitive and always learning * Demonstrate a high level of integrity and professionalism * Be a genuine team player, committed to supporting the team in achieving its goals **What we can offer you:** 🌍 Transparency: clear, fair salary bands and career levels 🏡 Hybrid work: 2–3 remote days \+ 20 days abroad per year 📚 Growth Mindset: 4 Growth Days, Growth Leave \+ training budget 🎁 Flexible benefits: personalize with health, wellbeing \& learning options 🌈 D\&I commitment: parental leave, D\&I Day, gender equity in rewards 🎉 Unbox Your World: Smartbox experience days \+ paid sabbatical after 5 years ✅ Essentials: healthcover, pension, extra leave, and more

title: Database Engineer, AWS DMS
description: **Description** AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) \- Database Engineer AWS Database Migration Service has migrated more than 1\.5 million workloads to AWS, helping enterprises move from commercial databases (Oracle, SQL Server, DB2\) to open\-source Aurora and RDS (PostgreSQL, MySQL). As a DMS Database Engineer, you are the front line for customer success. You diagnose complex replication and migration failures, propose database\-specific workarounds, and guide customers through production incidents involving CDC, schema migration, and data validation across heterogeneous database engines. You work at the intersection of deep database internals knowledge and customer\-facing problem solving \- turning ambiguous production failures into root causes and fixes. **What You'll Do** Diagnose and resolve complex customer escalations involving heterogeneous database migration and replication (Oracle CDC, SQL Server log reading, PostgreSQL logical decoding, MySQL binlog parsing). Perform root cause analysis on production incidents \- analyzing core dumps, task logs, metrics, and database behavior to identify whether issues originate in the source, target, or DMS engine. Design and validate migration strategies for large\-scale customer engagements, including data validation approaches, performance tuning, and cutover planning. Develop and maintain database\-specific test scenarios for our Scalable Test Platform (STP), improving coverage across migration paths and parameter combinations. Collaborate with SDEs to refine migration and replication engine behavior \- translating customer\-observed database edge cases into reproducible test cases and code fixes. Contribute to Pre\-Migration Assessment (PMA) rules that proactively detect configuration issues before customers begin migrations. Leverage and improve AI\-powered diagnostic tools (DMS Oncall Agent, Meshclaw) to accelerate troubleshooting and reduce time\-to\-resolution. Author runbooks, knowledge base articles, and escalation documentation that enable support teams and fellow engineers to resolve issues without service team engagement. Participate in on\-call rotation, triaging Sev\-2/Sev\-3 tickets and providing database\-expert guidance to support engineers. AWS DMS is a part of AWS Utility Computing (UC) which provides product innovations \- from foundational services such as Amazon’s Simple Storage Service (S3\) and Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2\), to consistently released new product innovations that continue to set AWS’s services and features apart in the industry. As a member of the UC organization, you’ll support the development and management of Compute, Database, Storage, Internet of Things (Iot), Platform, and Productivity Apps services in AWS, including support for customers who require specialized security solutions for their cloud services. Key job responsibilities Leverage deep expertise in database internals (storage engines, transaction logs, replication mechanisms, query optimization) across PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, or DB2 to diagnose and resolve complex production issues. Investigate and resolve customer\-facing database problems including performance degradation, data corruption, replication lag, CDC failures, and schema migration edge cases. Build automation, log analysis tools, and diagnostic scripts using SQL and scripting languages (Python, Bash, Perl) to accelerate troubleshooting and reduce manual investigation effort. Apply knowledge of database replication technologies \- CDC (Change Data Capture), logical replication, log\-based capture, and data validation \- to guide customers through migration and replication challenges. Collaborate with SDEs on root cause analysis by reading and reasoning about C/C\+\+ or Java code in the DMS replication engine to trace failures from customer symptoms to code\-level defects. Operate within the AWS ecosystem (RDS, Aurora, DMS, Redshift, S3, CloudWatch) to investigate issues end\-to\-end across infrastructure, networking, and database layers. Document complex technical findings clearly — writing root cause analyses, runbooks, and knowledge base articles that enable support teams, engineering peers, and customers to resolve issues independently. A day in the life As You Design And Code Solutions To Help Our Team Drive Efficiencies In Software Architecture, You’ll Create Metrics, Implement Automation And Other Improvements, And Resolve The Root Cause Of Software Defects. You’ll Also Build high\-impact solutions to deliver to our large customer base. Participate in design discussions, code review, and communicate with internal and external stakeholders. Work cross\-functionally to help drive business decisions with your technical input. Work in a startup\-like development environment, where you’re always working on the most important stuff. **About The Team** Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we're building an environment that celebrates knowledge\-sharing and mentorship. Our senior members enjoy one\-on\-one mentoring and thorough, but kind, code reviews. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects that help our team members develop your engineering expertise so you feel empowered to take on more complex tasks in the future. The DMS engine team operates across Dublin, Poland, and the US. We're a team that takes ownership of a complex system and makes it better every release \- reducing crashes, closing security gaps, and making migrations reliable enough that customers don't need our help to succeed. We leverage AI extensively: from autonomous bug\-fixing to an on\-call agent that cuts troubleshooting time dramatically. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent, or experience with database administration * Experience in relational database technology (such as Redshift, Oracle, MySQL or MS SQL) * Experience with at least one scripting language (shell, Python, Perl) and a procedural language for at least one database (PL/SQL, T\-SQL etc.) * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent * Deep understanding of data replication with hands\-on experience in at least one replication technology (CDC, logical replication, log shipping) **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of database design and implementation or equivalent * Experience with general troubleshooting/debugging of hardware, or experience debugging, profiling, and implementing best software engineering practices in large\-scale systems * Experience with automation and any version control tools, or experience with threat modeling and penetration testing and experience in SQL Server/MySQL * Experience supporting large\-scale database migrations (thousands of databases, multi\-tenant architectures). Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
