title: Digital Marketing Analyst
description: We’re currently working with one of Ireland’s leading digital and sports marketing agencies, they are looking for a Digital Marketing Analyst to join the team. The role will support analytics and performance marketing campaigns across major sports organisations including UEFA, FAI and IRFU This role is focused on analytics, tracking and campaign measurement across paid search and paid social activity. They’re looking for someone with strong hands\-on experience in GA4 and Google Tag Manager, who understands how digital campaigns are measured and optimised properly. **Responsibilities:** * Own GA4 reporting, conversion tracking and website analytics * Manage tagging and tracking implementation through Google Tag Manager * Set up and maintain tracking across paid search and paid social campaigns * Analyse campaign and user behaviour data to identify performance insights * Build dashboards and performance reports across digital marketing activity * Support CRM segmentation, audience tracking and automation workflows * Work closely with paid media, digital marketing and development teams to ensure accurate campaign measurement **What we're looking for:** * Experience in Digital Analytics / MarTech / Performance Marketing * Strong GA4 and Google Tag Manager experience * Good understanding of attribution and conversion tracking * Experience working across paid media campaigns * Exposure to CRM, segmentation or automation workflows. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys analytics, performance marketing and digital campaigns, while also wanting exposure to major sports organisations and large\-scale sporting campaigns.

title: Document Controller
description: Document Controller, Mercury Headquarters, Ballycoolin, Dublin Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Scope Of The Role** Mercury is currently looking for an experienced document controller to join our pre\-construction team. The role will be based in our head office in Dublin. We are looking for an enthusiastic and independent individual that can contribute to our team with deep knowledge and understanding of project document control, who can perform administrative tasks, and be open minded in implementing our information management procedures for our tendering department. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Coordinate and log incoming information to the department with our bid coordination team, including tender documents, events and meetings. * Coordinate and log outgoing communications from the department, including tender submission. * Set up the tender projects on our information management platforms. * Manage control of access to our information management platforms. * Produce key documents for tender information management such as document registers, contract document registers, tender close out reports. * Review documentation received and cross check them against the document registers. * Perform various administrative tasks within the department, such as SAP entries, requisitions. * Ensure compliance with ISO 19650 information management standards. * Maintain standard of procedure documents for the department. **Essential Criteria Of The Role** * 5\-10 years' experience with document control in construction projects. * Degree in administration. * Knowledge of ISO19650 standards. * Fluency in English is a requirement. * Common data environment application skillset. * Knowledge of CRM solutions; Dynamisc, Salesforce etc. **Desirable Criteria** * Knowledge of Bluebeam is an advantage. * Knowledge of BIM is an advantage. * Fluent communication in German or French is an advantage ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: EMEA Enterprise Technology Risk Management, Vice President
description: **Who We Are** Enterprise Technology Risk Management (ETRM) organization is part of Enterprise Risk Management organization in State Street Corporation (SSC). ETRM as Second Line of Defence (SLOD) is responsible for thought leadership, oversight, monitoring, and advisement around the discovery and remediation of Technology Risk and Cybersecurity Risk. **Who We Are Looking For** We seek a seasoned Technology and Cyber Risk Management Leader with over 10 years of experience in the financial services or technology sectors. The role will report to the EMEA Head of Enterprise Technology Risk Management. The ideal candidate will excel in identifying, managing, and monitoring technology and cyber risks through effective risk management processes, frameworks, and policies for the EMEA region. This role will involve communication with all levels of First Line teams, management, and senior executives in IT, Business Units, Enterprise Risk Management (ERM), and other corporate functions at both EMEA and global levels. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills are essential, along with the ability to convey technology risks to non\-technical audiences. Candidates should demonstrate strong initiative, perform well under pressure, and manage multiple diverse assignments. Prior experience with EMEA regulators (e.g., PRA, FCA and ECB) is highly desired. Experience in Cyber and Information Security, Cloud Risk Management (AWS, Azure), and Technology and Operational Resilience is required. This role can be performed in London, or Dublin, in hybrid model (4 days on site, 1 day remote). **Why this role is important to us** The ETRM team is critical to our organization's success. Our mission is to establish a world\-class Technology and Cyber Risk Management Oversight program that aligns business and technology strategies for effective decision\-making. As we undergo significant transformations and risk reduction initiatives, you will provide thought leadership and support to both ETRM peers and EMEA stakeholders (business and corporate areas) of the various jurisdictions. **What You Will Be Responsible For** As the ETRM advisor for the EMEA region, you will be responsible for: * Technology Risk and Cyber Risk Management + Lead and support functions within the ETRM Service Catalogue, including real\-time risk oversight, technology targeted risk assessments, Material Risk Identification, Regulatory Audit and Client Engagements, Risk Reporting. + Advise and/or support on technology risk and regulatory matters for the EMEA community. + Collaborate with ETRM Risk professionals to align with the broader Enterprise / Operational Risk Management Programs and mandates at global level. At regional level, develop and maintain EMEA relationships with Business and IT stakeholders. Partner with EMEA ERM team to assure the firm’s technology risks and non\-compliance are proactively identified, prudently managed (monitored and effectively challenged). + Develop effective communication channels for measuring and escalating technology risk exposure. + Evaluate IT Security risks arising from control inefficiencies in EMEA Legal Entities (LE). + Ensure reporting is properly balanced between perspectives of global ETRM opinion and the local Authorities. + Participate in due diligence for new clients, vendors and M\&A activities. + Monitor emerging technology risks and trends in financial services. + Deliver assignments and projects independently and on time. + Prepare presentations for Management, Risk committees and Board meetings. * Governance and Oversight + Oversee governance, policy and framework execution across the EMEA region, ensuring alignment with global frameworks. + Support the development of technology risk oversight and embed the ETRM and practices. + Oversee communication with EMEA regulators in alignment with the ETRM program. + Report with appropriate and timely information for the committees/Boards to effectively discharge their responsibilities * Risk Excellence and Education/Awareness + Foster a culture of effective challenge throughout the organization. + Provide consulting on technology risk management and ongoing guidance aligned with ETRM strategy. + Stayed informed on regulatory developments and their impacts on State Street in EMEA. + Conduct training on technology risk management **What We Value** * Collaborative approach to maximize positive impact and synergy * Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to influence and implement decisions * Flexibility to accommodate business hours across EMEA and global teams. * Strategic mindset to connect various aspects and initiatives for a holistic risk and control environment * Ability to be a strong voice for review and challenge while continuing to maintain positive relationships with stakeholders * Strong multitasking skills and ability to navigate competing priorities * Effective relationships management across diverse cultural groups **Primary Skills (Must Have)** * In\-depth knowledge of EMEA Technology Regulatory Requirements (e.g., FCA, PRA, ECB); Experience with regulatory exams and responses is strongly desired. * Experience in EEA regulations such as DORA, BCBS 239 and the EU AI Act. * Experience in IT audits or risk assessments, Information Technology General Controls (ITGC) and cybersecurity controls. Familiarity with Information Security Frameworks (e.g., NIST, ISO 27000, CSA Cloud Control Matrix) and ITIL practices * Experience in AI adoption and AI risk management * Ability to articulate technical issues to non\-IT stakeholders and business perspective to IT stakeholders * Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills, with cross\-cultural competencies **Education \& Preferred Qualifications** * Minimum 10 years of experience in the financial and/or technology industries. * Strong project management, critical thinking, problem\-solving, and decision\-making abilities. * Experience in IT risk management, compliance or audit, including control framework design \& implementation. * Professional IT certifications, e.g., CGEIT, CISA, CISM, CISSP, CCSP, COBIT, CRISC and ITIL would be advantageous. * Experience with data analytics and GRC tools, including Tableau and Power BI, is a plus. * Undergraduate or advanced degree in a technology discipline **About State Street** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement Job ID: R\-789994

title: ESG & Systems Coordinator
description: **KPRO \- Brownstown, Kilcullen, Co. Kildare, R56 VR86** As Ireland's leading manufacturer of building materials, Kilsaran see's itself at the forefront of innovation and sustainable practices. Environmental, Social, Governance values are at the core of our business and is a growing team within our group. We are seeking an ESG \& Systems Coordinator to support the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of the organisation's Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) strategy. The role focuses on environmental performance, sustainability initiatives, compliance with regulatory requirements, and alignment with ISO\-based management systems. **Responsibilities** **ESG Strategy \& Reporting** * Support the delivery of the company's ESG strategy and sustainability roadmap * Collect, analyse, and report ESG performance data (e.g. carbon emissions, energy use, waste, water) * Assist in the preparation of ESG disclosures (e.g. annual sustainability reports, VSME, and CSRD where applicable) * Monitor ESG trends, standards, and regulatory requirements **Environmental Management** * Support environmental impact reduction initiatives (carbon, waste, biodiversity, resource efficiency) * Track environmental KPIs and ensure targets are monitored and updated * Assist with carbon footprint calculations and sustainability metrics * Promote environmental awareness and best practices across the organisation **ISO Management Systems** * Maintain and improve Environmental Management Systems (EMS) aligned with ISO 14001 * Support integrated management systems where applicable (e.g. ISO 9001, ISO 45001, ISO 50001\) * Assist with internal and external ISO audits, including documentation and corrective actions * Ensure compliance with environmental policies, procedures, and legal obligations **Compliance \& Governance** * Maintain environmental compliance registers and ensure adherence to local and international legislation * Support internal governance processes, ESG risk assessments, and controls * Coordinate ESG\-related policies and procedures **Stakeholder Engagement** * Collaborate with internal teams (operations, procurement, HR, finance) to embed ESG practices * Support communication of ESG performance to stakeholders (employees, customers, suppliers) * Assist with supplier sustainability assessments and engagement initiatives **Qualifications \& Experience** * A third\-level degree in environmental science or other relevant discipline. **Skills \& Abilities** * Good mathematical and numerical skills. * Effective written and verbal communication skills. * Proven I.T. skills, including strong knowledge of Microsoft Word, Outlook, Excel etc. * Can work on your own initiative, researching ESG trends etc., but also willing and able to work as part of a team.

title: Electrical Engineer- Dublin
description: Electrical Engineer Engineering is broken. Help rebuild it . Too many engineers are stuck executing instead of thinking. At IN2, you'll be expected to challenge, contribute, and influence, not just deliver. The Role You’ll work across the full project lifecycle, designing electrical systems and influencing decisions from day one. This isn’t just delivery, you’ll be involved in shaping solutions, working directly with teams and clients, and driving projects forward. What You'll Do * Design electrical systems including power, lighting, and life safety * Produce detailed calculations, specifications, and reports * Support projects from early\-stage concept through to completion * Coordinate with mechanical teams and wider stakeholders * Attend design and client meetings, contributing to solutions * Ensure compliance with regulations and best practice * Support site inspections and commissioning phases What You'll Bring * Degree in Electrical or Building Services Engineering * 2 to 5\+ years' experience in electrical design * Strong understanding of building services systems and standards * Experience with Amtech, Dialux, or similar software * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Strong communication and problem\-solving skills What You'll Get This isn't built around perks. It's built around how high\-performing engineers actually want to work. Real ownership, real progression, and a culture that trusts you to deliver. *The Essentials* * Competitive salary, bonus and pension that reflect your impact * 22 to 27 days annual leave, plus bank holidays * Flexible working across a 37\.5 hour week: early start or later start? you decide *Career Progression* * A clear path forward, with responsibility increasing as you prove it * Direct exposure to senior leaders, not layers of hierarchy * IN2 Academy, CPD support and chartership guidance * Fully paid professional fees *Health and Wellbeing* * Employee Assistance Programme and annual health checks * Wellbeing and fitness initiatives that support you long term * Cycle to Work scheme to make the everyday easier *Social and Community* * Genuinely strong team culture, built through regular events and shared wins * Employee\-led charity initiatives with real involvement * Offices in Dublin, London, Berlin, Belfast, Athlone and Madrid Why IN2? Across every role, electrical, mechanical, or BIM, the IN2 difference is the same: you're expected to think, lead, and shape outcomes, not just execute instructions. Here's what sets us apart. Ownership from Day One Whether you're an Engineer or a Senior, you'll have real accountability and decision\-making authority. No waiting for permission. No layers of sign\-off slowing you down. Progression Based on Ability Faster progression based on what you deliver, not how long you've been in the seat. A clear pathway from Engineer to Senior, Associate, and beyond. Innovation Time Dedicated time to develop new ideas, explore emerging technologies, and contribute to sustainability strategies: built into how we work, not bolted on. High\-Performance, Low\-Ego Culture A team that challenges each other, supports each other, and wins together. No hierarchy for hierarchy's sake. Just talented people doing exceptional work. Complex, High\-Profile Projects Exposure to design\-led, high\-impact developments that push the boundaries of what building services engineering can achieve. Global Reach Work across offices in Dublin, London, Berlin, Belfast, Athlone, and Madrid\- a genuinely international practice with a collaborative, connected culture. *Ready to own the outcome? Join a team that expects more and gives more in return.*

title: Estimating Assistant (Entry-Level)
description: We are looking for a motivated and organised person to join our team as an Estimating Assistant. This is a junior, entry\-level position with full in\-house training provided. The role would suit a school leaver, college graduate, or someone looking to start a long\-term career in the construction industry. No previous estimating experience is required — we are looking for someone with a positive attitude, willingness to learn, and good attention to detail. You will support our estimating department with quotations, supplier pricing, tender administration, and general office duties while learning the role from experienced team members. If interested, send your CV to admin@dsventilation.ie or message directly. What You’ll Learn * Reviewing drawings and tender documents * Assisting with quantity take\-offs * Requesting supplier and subcontractor quotations * Preparing pricing documents and schedules * Managing project and tender information * Supporting the estimating team with day\-to\-day administration * Attending site visits and meetings when required What We’re Looking For * Strong computer skills * Good communication and numeracy skills * Organised and willing to learn * Ability to work as part of a team * Good attention to detail * Positive attitude and reliable work ethic Important This is a trainee/junior role and full training will be provided. Previous estimating or construction industry experience is not required. Work Location: In person

title: Executive Assistant - Business Administration
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Internal Firm Services \- Other **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** PwC Ireland\- Bring Your Talent, Grow Your Skills, Unlock Your Potential PwC offers an exciting opportunity to be part of a fast\-growing team of Executive Assistants (EAs) within the Core Administration Hub. Supported in part by the Business Administration Hubs, the EA will provide strategic and professional senior administrative support to designated Firm Partner(s). PwC’s Business Enablement Services (BES) Line of Service combines our support teams from across the firm who play pivotal roles in enabling our business delivery. Within BES, the Business Administration (BA) function delivers innovative workplace and business\-focused solutions across the firm through our collaborative team of experts. Our team ensures the effective operation and support of client service teams is in safe hands. We are passionate about customer service and deliver a wide range of services, from core administrative tasks to value\-add work for our stakeholders. BA is composed of four Hubs – Core Administration, Commercial Operations, Events and Travel, and Documentation. **This is a fantastic opportunity for the right individual to;** * Be an integral part of the Business Administration team providing dedicated support to Partners * Proactively develop their personal and technical skills to enhance their career * Expand their networks across PwC * Significantly broaden their understanding of the firm To be successful you will have the ability to liaise, influence and challenge effectively to build strong networks and relationships at all levels, both internally and externally. You will report into a Team Leader and will have a Career Coach to support your career aspirations and personal development. You will drive your own self development through gaining continuous feedback, evidencing an active commitment to upskilling in the role and taking ownership to plan and attend regular Career Coach Catch Ups. The role requires a high\-level of self\-management and personal responsibility coupled with the ability to prioritise, organise and manage tasks and workload. You will have a customer service mindset, good technical skills and the ability to keep up to date with current and emerging technology. **Purpose\-led work you’ll be part of** As an Associate, you’ll work as part of a team of problem solvers, helping to solve complex business issues from strategy to execution. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: **Responsible for:** * Management of an executive\-level BA service for Partners * Ownership of all enquiries and problems, resolving or escalating issues as appropriate. * Work with your stakeholders to ensure compliance with the Firm’s policies and procedures and meet the Firm deadlines. * Draft complex and sensitive communications on behalf of partners. * Proactively coordinate meeting logistics and anticipate conflicts. * Attend meetings as appropriate, providing detailed and actionable minutes. * Manage follow\-up on key action items and ensure accountability. * Project coordination \- attend project meetings (as requested) to understand requirements, delivery timescales, supporting actions and regular interaction with the project team. * Manage partner inboxes, prioritising critical communications. Handle sensitive documents with a high level of confidentiality and integrity. * Build and maintain strong client relationships, addressing issues proactively. * Liaise with the BA Hubs and Regional Offices as necessary. * Delegate transactional work where appropriate to effectively manage workloads. * Ability to draft stakeholder emails and engage with stakeholders to make decisions. * Support stakeholders and others in upskilling of Firmwide systems. **Additional responsibilities where required:** * Informal coaching junior team members * Involvement in team activities that contribute to personal \& Firm development * Other ad hoc responsibilities as required **Essential skills \& experience** * Secondary level education and a relevant Post second level qualification. * A minimum of 4 years of proven experience in a similar role, providing comprehensive administrative support to senior executives or management teams. * Proficiency in Microsoft 365 applications (Word, Outlook, PowerPoint, Excel). * Demonstrated strong project management and coordination skills, with the ability to effectively prioritise and manage multiple stakeholders' conflicting priorities. * Experience in handling sensitive information. * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment, demonstrating resilience and adaptability. * Demonstrated champion of Evolved PwC Professional behaviours. **Preferred Experience** * Excellent organisational skills and self\-motivation, demonstrating a commitment to continuous self\-development. * Strong influencing and communication skills, both verbal and written. * Ability to develop internal and external networks to support business objectives. * Flexibility and agility to adapt to changes and contribute effectively to system improvements or firm initiatives. * Role model for inclusivity, with an understanding of cultural differences and a commitment to diversity. **Unlock your potential with PwC Ireland** We believe that challenges are better solved together! We’re inspiring and empowering our people to change the world. Powered by the latest technology, you’ll be a part of amazing teams encouraged to collaborate and innovate in a way that creates powerful solutions and makes a positive impact. This purpose\-led work, and our continuous development and encouragement, will help unlock your potential and take your career to the next level. **Enjoy PwC’s perks** We reward your impact, and support your wellbeing, through a competitive compensation package, inclusive employee benefits and flexibility programs that will help you thrive in work and life. Learn more about us at Life@PwC. (https://www.pwc.ie/careers\-ie/life\-at\-pwc.html). **Being appreciated for being you** Our most valuable asset is our people and we grow stronger as we learn from one another. We are an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. title: Executive Assistant: International Government Affairs
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Corporate Functions Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Job Description: Executive Assistant (International Government Affairs)** --------------------------------------------------------------------------- **Department Description:** The International Government Affairs team is a diverse, high\-impact cohort of professionals managing the company’s strategic engagement with government officials, legislators, and regulators throughout EMEA, APAC, and ASEAN. Integrated within this group, the Global Public Policy team serves as a critical engine for shaping our corporate positions on evolving public policy issues. **Role Description:** You will serve as the strategic partner to the Vice President of International Government Affairs (EMEA, APAC, ASEAN) and Global Public Policy. Your mission is to maximize leadership impact by navigating complex internal ecosystems, orchestrating global logistics, and ensuring a distributed 20\-person team remains synchronized across three continents. Beyond traditional calendar management, you will own the operational rhythm of a high\-velocity global department. **Core Responsibilities:** **1\. Internal Navigation \& Approvals** * **The "Fixer":** Help manage all internal approval proceedings (contracts, headcount, budget requests, etc.). * **Expense Management:** Own the end\-to\-end expense process, ensuring accuracy across different currencies and compliance with company policy. **2\. Communication Architecture** * **Information Flow:** Ensure that key updates from the manager reach the team, and that urgent "bottom\-up" information reaches the manager. * **Channels Management:** Structure and manage how the team communicates and collaborates with the other teams. This includes optimizing Slack channels, managing email distribution lists, and ensuring docs are organized and accessible. * **Cross\-Functional Bridge:** Act as a connector between the Government Affairs team and other departments (Legal, PR, Finance) to ensure everyone is in the loop. Help develop a more cross\-functional approach across the team, aiming to leverage existing resources, build on existing expertise, and share accurate information to maximize impact and velocity. **3\. Global Operations \& Logistics** * **Agenda** : Manage a complex agenda by combining a high volume of internal and external stakeholder meetings, overseeing relationships with highly sensitive and multi cultural stakeholders (government officials, C\-Suite level), and helping to prioritize and manage agenda pressure. * **Travel** **Coordinate:** complex, multi\-leg international trips. You’ll handle the logistics (flights, cars, hotels). * **Time Zone Logic:** Manage a high\-volume calendar across EMEA, APAC, and ASEAN. You understand the "golden hours" where these regions overlap and protect those slots for critical syncs. **4\. Team Coordination** * **Team Pulse:** Organise team meetings and offsites that actually work for a distributed group. * **Project Liaison:** Work alongside our Cross\-Functional Project Manager. While they track the "policy wins," you track the "people and process," ensuring the Leader is prepared for every milestone. **Minimum Requirements:** * Minimum of 5\+ years of applicable administrative experience, ideally from a global software organization. * Skillful execution of administrative activities, with high attention to detail, organization and process. * Experience with multi\-cultural teams. * Strong teamwork skills; and a confident ability to offer suggestions and improvements to process and work very collaboratively with others. * Strong relationship management skills, ability to build a personal network throughout the company. * Strong verbal and written communication. Ability to communicate clearly and effectively with senior leaders and external partners. * Proven track record of supporting executive leadership within dynamic, high\-growth environments, including high\-stakes engagement with senior public and private sector stakeholders. * Working proficiency of Google Suite (Gmail, Docs, Drive, GMeet). Experience using Quip, Slack, Salesforce and Tableau and are a plus. * Must be in one of the listed locations and open to being hybrid in office 3 days per week. **Additional Desired Skills/Traits** * Customer centric orientation. * Discreet \& Diplomatic: You handle sensitive information regarding government negotiations and internal ELT discussions with total confidentiality. * Agility and flexibility in response to changing priorities and needs. * Proven ability to manage projects, work under pressure and consistently make deadlines. * Demonstrate ability and willingness to continuously acquire new competencies and accept new challenges. * Possess an ability and curiosity and to comprehend the global DNA of the company and its organizations. * Ensure communications are clear, thorough, and specific, with attention to follow\-through. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. title: Financial Analyst - Accounts Payable
description: Job description: **Financial Analyst \- Accounts Payable** **Based in Dublin** **\*\*Closing Date for Applications \- Friday 29th May\*\*** **Overview** We are seeking a commercially minded and process\-driven Accounts Payable Analyst to support our Events \& Media functions. This role will be based in our Dublin office and will act as a key liaison between Finance and the business, ensuring efficient, scalable, and well\-controlled financial operations. The successful candidate will bring strong experience from a multinational or shared services environment, with a proven track record in accounts payable, expenses, and process improvement. This is an opportunity to play a pivotal role in enhancing finance operations while partnering closely with stakeholders across a dynamic, global organisation. **TASKS \& RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Partner with the World Rugby business teams in Dublin to handle their purchasing needs, coordinate with suppliers, and resolve all payment‑processing queries. * Act as trusted partner to internal teams, applying a customer‑centric approach to build strong relationships and deliver high‑quality support. * Proactively deal with large volume of invoices and / or suppliers’ correspondences with accuracy and speed, ensuring all invoices are processed by month end and payment run deadlines. * Manage end\-to\-end invoice processing and execute payment runs as required, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely settlement. * Ensure all foreign VAT figures are posted correctly and providing backup to the Accounts Assistant to reclaim * Lead supplier onboarding processes, ensuring compliance with internal controls, governance standards, and audit requirements * Serve as the primary point of contact for supplier queries, promoting efficient and consistent onboarding practices across the business * Partner with the wider finance team including query resolution, reporting, insights, cashflow queries, and supplier reconciliations to support month\-end close * Support internal and external audit processes, ensuring timely and accurate responses to audit queries * Drive continuous improvement initiatives, identifying opportunities to enhance efficiency, automation, and control across finance operations * Contribute to finance transformation projects, including system implementations and process enhancements (e.g., bank documentation workflows) **SKILLS \& EXPERIENCE:** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience in a finance operations or accounts payable role within a multinational organisation or shared services environment * A team player with a professional conduct and problem\-solving skills and who has a proactive, ‘can\-do’ approach. As well as the ability to work independently. * Experience of implementation of new purchasing tools/ invoice scanning (preferred) * In\-depth knowledge of end\-to\-end Purchase\-to\-Pay processes, with a track record of applying best practice to drive efficient and accuracy. * Qualification or part\-qualification in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration, or a related discipline preferred * Strong working knowledge of ERP systems (SAP preferred) Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel * Experience across centralised finance processes (AP, expenses, corporate cards, or P2P) * Strong analytical, organisational, and problem\-solving skills with high attention to detail * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills ***Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion:*** *World Rugby is committed to building a diverse workforce and therefore strongly encourages applications from underrepresented groups. We are committed to equality and inclusion and welcome applications from all individuals, regardless of their background. We strive to be a diverse and inclusive workplace, which reflects the global sport we want to be and where everyone can be themselves and feel they belong.* ***Flexible Working:*** *Most of our roles offer hybrid working. We ask colleagues to work :* *Three (3\) days a week “out of home”. Two (2\) “core” office days (Tuesday \& Wednesday) and a third day of your choice to be with stakeholders.* *“Out of home” can mean working in a World Rugby office/hub, working from a venue or face to face meetings with partners/suppliers/agencies/teammates or other rugby family stakeholders.* *The remaining two (2\) days are flexible to work from home (or the office)\>* *This applies to our Dublin \& London offices.*

title: Financial Analyst - Group Finance
description: Financial Analyst – Group Reporting (Graduate) Location – Dublin (2 days Hybrid) 12 month FTC Flutter consists of two commercial divisions (FanDuel and International) and our central Flutter Functions; COO, Finance \& Legal. Here in Flutter Functions we work with colleagues across all our divisions and regions to deliver something we call the Flutter Edge. It’s our competitive advantage, our ‘secret sauce’ which plays a key part in our ongoing success and powers our brands and divisions, through Product, Tech, Expertise and Scale. In Flutter Finance we pride ourselves in providing global expertise to ensure Flutter is financially healthy. Utilising our Flutter Edge to turbo\-charge our capabilities. **Job Description** We are looking for a Financial Analyst (Graduate) to join our Group Accounting \& Reporting (GA\&R) team at Flutter. As a Financial Analyst, you will play an important role in assisting the GA\&R team with its monthly management reporting requirements and with the preparation of the annual and quarterly U.S. GAAP financial statements and the annual IFRS report. You will have the opportunity to collaborate with various teams and actively contribute to the Group’s accurate accounting and reporting. **What You’ll Do** * Support the month\-end close process within Group deadlines for assigned channels of the GA\&R function, including the preparation and posting of journal entries * Assist with the preparation of various sections within quarterly and annual reports, in accordance with U.S. GAAP and SEC rules and regulations. * Assist with the preparation of the annual report, in accordance with IFRS. * Support divisional finance teams, financial planning and analysis (FP\&A) teams with ad\-hoc accounting, analytical and financial reporting matters. * Work with team members to document accounting processes and internal controls, as needed. * Maintain compliance with SOX requirements, including assisting in certain tasks to comply with SOX controls, as needed. * Support ad\-hoc activities and initiatives, as needed. **How You’ll Do It** * Must hold a recognised accounting/finance degree * Ability to manage multiple tasks and drive adherence to deadlines. * Strong communication skills. * Strong research, analytical and problem\-solving skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Prior accounting/financial reporting experience is preferred * Experience of Oracle ERP or other similar financial systems is an advantage. * Excellent MS Office skills, particularly in Excel. **What’s In It For You** We are a flexible employer; whether you have personal commitments or a hobby that brings you joy, we want you to bring your best self to work and feel empowered to do so. We also like to share our success; after all you make it happen. We have an excellent benefits package that can be personalised to you: * Bonus scheme * Uncapped holiday allowance * Enhanced pension scheme * Private healthcare * Life assurance * Income protection * Hybrid working * £1,000 annual self\-development learning fund * Invest via the Flutters Sharesave Scheme * Paid volunteering days * Enhanced parental leave * Wellbeing fund (£/€250 a year) * Recognition programs **About Flutter** We are a world leader in online sports betting and iGaming, with a market leading position in the US and across the world. We have an unparalleled portfolio of the most innovative, diverse and distinctive brands including FanDuel, Sky Betting \& Gaming, Sportsbet, PokerStars, Paddy Power, Sisal, tombola, Betfair, MaxBet and Adjarabet. With our global scale and challenger attitude, through which we excite and entertain our customers, in a safe and sustainable way. Using our collective power, the Flutter Edge, we aim to disrupt the sector, learning from the past to create a better future for our customers, colleagues and communities. We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role. If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.

title: Full Stack Cybercrime Operator
description: **This job is with eBay, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ\+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.** At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader \- we're changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We're committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work \- every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers \- and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. ****Full Stack Cybercime Operator**** ****Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Employment Type:** Full Time, In Office **Company:** eBay** ****About the team**** At eBay, we build systems that protect customers from cybercrime and abuse. This team turns cybercrime research into production\-grade signals, automations, services, and protections. The work spans off\-platform cybercime discovery and on\-platform prevention, with a strong emphasis on engineering systems that move quickly from insight to action. ****The role**** We are hiring a **Full Stack Cybercrime Operator** to build and deploy the systems that help identify, investigate, and disrupt cybercrime targeting eBay and its customers. This is an **engineering\-led role** . We are looking for a strong software engineer who can build production systems in **Python, Java, and SQL** , operate effectively in the cloud, and ship reliable capabilities at speed. At the same time, this role requires a **critical security research mindset** . You need to think like an attacker, understand how cybercrime campaigns work, and apply that mindset to the design of better signals, automations, workflows, and protections. This is not a pure research role, but strong security research instincts are essential. We also expect **Agentic Engineering and AI capability** to be a core strength. You should be able to design and build agentic systems, AI\-enabled workflows, and autonomous or semi\-autonomous tooling that improves the speed, scale, and quality of cybercrime protections. ****What you will do**** * Build production systems in Python, Java, and SQL that detect, investigate, and respond to cybercrime and abuse. * Design and deploy agentic systems, AI\-powered workflows, and automation tooling for cybercrime research, enrichment, and protection. * Build services, APIs, pipelines, and internal tools that convert cybercrime intelligence into scalable customer protections. * Investigate phishing, brand abuse, digital identity crime, and related cybercrime patterns, then translate findings into durable engineering solutions. * Develop and improve signals, decisioning logic, and protection workflows that measurably reduce abuse and improve response speed. * Work with cloud technologies and distributed systems to deploy reliable, secure, and scalable capabilities. * Partner with security, product, and engineering teams to move from cybercime identification to shipped protection with minimal handoffs. * Own systems end to end: design, build, test, deploy, monitor, and improve. * Maintain a high quality bar, including rapid response to production issues and strong operational discipline. ****What success looks like**** **Success In This Role Is Defined By Concrete Outcomes** * You build systems that shorten the time from cybercime discovery to deployed protection. * You deliver engineering solutions that improve prevention, identification, disruption, or remediation at scale. * You apply a security research mindset to uncover attacker behavior and convert those insights into production capabilities. * You build AI\-native and agentic capabilities that reduce manual effort and improve speed, coverage, and consistency. * You ship reliable systems with strong security, quality, and operational ownership. ****Minimum qualifications**** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Security, or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience. * Strong software engineering experience building and operating production systems. * Strong coding skills in Python and Java. * Strong SQL skills. * Hands\-on experience with cloud technologies and modern distributed systems. * Experience building APIs, backend services, pipelines, automation tooling, or data\-driven applications in production. * Experience debugging, monitoring, and improving systems in production. * Ability to translate ambiguous problems into concrete technical solutions. ****Preferred qualifications**** * Experience in security engineering, cybercrime investigations, intelligence, trust and safety, fraud, abuse prevention, or adversarial research. * A strong security research / hacker mindset: curiosity, technical depth, and the ability to understand attacker behavior, test assumptions, and identify practical weaknesses or opportunities for disruption. * Experience investigating phishing, impersonation, credential theft, brand abuse, or digital identity attacks. * Experience designing and building agentic systems, AI workflows, LLM\-enabled tooling, or autonomous research and response capabilities. * Experience building detection systems, enrichment pipelines, or automated investigative workflows. * Experience working in fast\-moving environments where engineers own delivery from concept through production. ****What we are looking for**** * A strong engineer first. * Someone with a real security research mindset, not just surface\-level security interest. * Someone who can think like a hacker and build like a product engineer. * Someone comfortable using AI and agentic engineering as core tools, not side experiments. * Someone who takes ownership, moves with urgency, and ships high\-quality systems. ****Why join eBay**** This is an opportunity to build high\-impact security capabilities in a domain where the landscape changes constantly. You will work on systems that combine engineering, cybercime research, cloud infrastructure, and AI\-native automation to protect customers at scale. The direction of the team is toward building a cybercrime capability where automated systems continuously improve how cybercime is identified and protections are deployed. **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com . We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice , Privacy Center , and AI Hiring Guidelines . ]]\>

title: Fund Accounting (Alternatives) Associate 2
description: **Who we are looking for:** YOU! A dynamic and enthusiastic individual who is looking to work in an exciting and evolving sector. As part of our current expansion of operations in Ireland, we have a role at graduate and entry level available within our Alternatives Fund Accounting Group where you can enhance you career through experience gained in multiple facets including: * Private Markets Funds Join us if making your mark in the Alternatives Servicing Sector is a challenge you are up for. **What we can offer you:** We are the largest Fund Administrator in Ireland, servicing global market leading clients across a variety of product types. With us, you have the opportunity to develop your career in many different directions within the funds industry as well as within the organization as a whole. This is a team providing a crucial operational service for clients worldwide, and you will work with a range of internal teams across the globe to service a wide array of prominent clients across multiple fund types giving you a unique learning experience. We offer flexibility to blend working from home or office, excellent benefits package, strong personal and career development programs to support your career journey and much more. **As a Fund Accounting Associate, you will be involved in:** * Preparing daily cash, position and market value reconciliations including trade capture and pricing. * Assist in the Dealing Date accounting process. * Work on complex financial instruments such as equities, fixed income, futures options and swaps. * Participate in client related or internally driven projects * Prepare clients’ related correspondence (e.g. client requests, audit confirmations) * Assist Managers in semi\-annual and annual audit process. * Identify any unusual or potentially suspicious transaction activity and report and/or escalate in accordance with corporate policy and guidelines detailed in relevant operating procedures. * Assist in reviewing and resolving reconciliations of portfolio cash and security accounts. * Work on corporate actions, equities, fixed income, futures, options and swaps and complex financial instruments. * Assist in semi\-annual and annual audit process. * Handle more complex tasks. **What we value:** These skills will help you succeed in this role: * Strong analytical skills and an attention to detail and accuracy. * Strong problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work to tight deadlines. * Ability to escalate where appropriate. **Education \& Preferred Qualifications:** * Degree level at any discipline is preferred, but not essential. * 1–2 years of relevant experience would be an added advantage **About State Street** ====================== Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement

title: GTM Data & Performance Analyst
description: **About Smartbox:** Created in 2003, Smartbox is the French and European number 1 in experience gifts, with 6\.5 million boxes sold each year, around €350 million annual turnover, in a gift box market, offering the widest range of experience gifts. For more than 20 years, we have been developing thousands of experiences to offer and an equal number of unforgettable moments to live. From the most iconic to the most unique, Smartbox's mission has always been to enable everyone to gift their loved ones the opportunity to live unique experiences covering the themes of stay, adventures, well\-being and gastronomy. In the short future, we want to be the point of reference when it comes to inspiring people to live experiences. We are at a tipping point in terms of innovation in our market. The group employs 800 collaborators based across Europe. Smartbox gift boxes are sold in 10 European countries: France, Italy, Spain, Belgium, Switzerland, the Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden, Portugal and Germany. Pioneer in the sector and number 1 in most of these countries, significantly ahead of its competitors, the group relies on a network of approximately 150 partner retailers throughout Europe (18,000 major points of sale), and distributes its products as well as to the websites of the various brands of the group and with online distributors such as Amazon. One of the keys to the group's success is having built a network of 30,000 partners in Europe. The Group has been owned since 2013 by Otium Capital, which has been able to support more than 50 start\-ups and innovative companies thanks to the profits generated by the Smartbox Group. More than just a box, Smartbox has been one of the biggest success stories in the industry in the last 20 years. **About Your Role:** As a GTM Data \& Performance Analyst, you will support the GTM Strategy and Sales teams by producing reliable analyses, reports and dashboards that help monitor partner performance and commercial activity. This is an execution\-focused analytical role. You will work on clearly defined topics and priorities, contribute to recurring reporting and analyses, and help ensure that commercial teams have access to accurate and consistent data to support decision\-making. You will collaborate closely with GTM Strategy, RevOps and Sales, and interact regularly with Product, Tech and Finance to provide data inputs and insights. **Key Responsibilities:** **Data Analysis \& Partner Performance** * Produce recurring reports and analyses on partner performance across predefined dimensions such as partner tier, product category and geography * Monitor key performance indicators (e.g. revenue contribution, availability, catalog quality, NPS) and highlight notable variations or data issues * Prepare structured analyses to support reviews of partner performance, under the guidance of GTM Strategy * Support quarterly and monthly business reviews by preparing data extracts, charts and summaries * Track sales performance against targets for Account Managers and BDMs, and prepare standard reports for GTM leadership **Pricing \& Commercial Analysis Support** * Support pricing and margin analyses by preparing data inputs, simulations and comparison reports * Assist in modelling the commercial impact of predefined pricing or incentive scenarios * Help monitor pricing consistency across markets and categories and flag anomalies to relevant stakeholders * Provide analytical support for new commercial initiatives (e.g. new offer types or partner models), based on clearly defined scopes **Dashboards \& Reporting** * Maintain and update existing GTM dashboards used by GTM Strategy, Sales teams and country stakeholders * Ensure data accuracy and consistency across reports and dashboards * Implement dashboard changes and updates based on documented business requirements * Support the adoption of dashboards by commercial teams by helping explain metrics and reports **Marketplace \& Product Data Support** * Develop a good understanding of Smartbox’s marketplace and catalog performance metrics * Track predefined catalog health indicators and prepare reports for Product and Operations teams * Provide data and analysis to support cross\-functional discussions on marketplace performance when requested * Assist in compiling competitor and market data to support GTM analyses **Requirements for the Role:** * 2–4 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence or a related analytical role * Good working knowledge of SQL and at least one BI or data visualisation tool (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Metabase, Power BI) * Basic understanding of commercial, sales or marketplace environments * Ability to work with structured datasets and follow established analytical frameworks * Comfortable collaborating with multiple teams (Sales, GTM Strategy, RevOps, Product, Finance) * Strong attention to detail and data quality **Skills:** * Ability to execute data analyses accurately and on time * Structured and methodical approach to problem solving * Ability to summarise data and findings clearly * Good communication skills, with the ability to explain metrics and reports to non\-technical stakeholders * Willingness to learn and develop analytical and commercial skills * Adaptability in a dynamic business environment **What we value:** * **Reliability:** you deliver high\-quality analyses that teams can trust * **Curiosity:** you are eager to understand the business and how data supports it * **Collaboration:** you work effectively with different teams and functions * **Growth mindset:** you are motivated to develop your skills and take on increasing responsibility over time

title: General Operative
