title: Technical Support Professional – IBM Sovereign Core
description: **Introduction** At IBM Software, we transform client challenges into solutions. Building the world’s leading AI\-powered, cloud\-native products that shape the future of business and society. Our legacy of innovation creates endless opportunities for IBMers to learn, grow, and make an impact on a global scale. Working in Software means joining a team fueled by curiosity and collaboration. You’ll work with diverse technologies, partners, and industries to design, develop, and deliver solutions that power digital transformation. With a culture that values innovation, growth, and continuous learning, IBM Software places you at the heart of IBM’s product and technology landscape. Here, you’ll have the tools and opportunities to advance your career while creating software that changes the world. **Your Role And Responsibilities** Role Overview The IBM Sovereign Core Technical Support Professional plays a critical role in supporting secure, compliant, and customer‑operated environments built on IBM’s Sovereign Core software stack. In this position, you ensure the reliability, resilience, and smooth operation of mission‑critical workloads running within strict sovereign boundaries. You will troubleshoot complex technical issues, support deployments in regulated and air‑gapped environments, and act as a trusted advisor for customers adopting IBM Sovereign Core. **Responsibilities** * Manage support cases end‑to‑end—from triage through resolution—diagnosing and resolving issues across Sovereign Core components, including the control plane, embedded security services, identity systems, and AI/ML inference runtimes running on Red Hat OpenShift. * Support Sovereign Core deployments across on‑premises, air‑gapped, regulated environments, ensuring all work adheres to sovereignty, data‑residency, and operational‑control requirements. * Provide timely communication within a 24x7x365 support framework, coordinating incident response and mitigating issues affecting mission‑critical workloads. * Act as a customer advocate, identifying and escalating pain points, providing guidance, and contributing to improvements that enhance IBM Sovereign Core reliability, resiliency, and sovereign assurance. As part of maintaining continuous service coverage, the role includes participation in weekend standby and shift rotations for handling out‑of‑hours incidents. Due to the nature of this role and the associated client requirements, which include working with EU‑based customers in a sovereign environment, applicants must, by the job start date, lawfully reside in, be legally authorized to work in, and perform the role exclusively within the EU country of employment. **Preferred Education** Master's Degree **Required Technical And Professional Expertise** Technical Skills * Hands\-on experience with Red Hat OpenShift or Kubernetes platform administration. * Familiarity with compliance‑driven operations (e.g., audit trails, policy enforcement, governance frameworks). * Understanding of AI/ML inference pipelines, GPU scheduling, and model deployment workflows on container platforms. * Experience with encryption, secret management, and confidential computing concepts. Soft Skills * Demonstrate a growth mindset and ability to learn new technologies in a fast‑paced environment. * Strong communicator who can translate complex technical topics for a range of audiences and represent customer needs. * Problem\-solving mindset with ability to navigate technical and business constraints. * Collaborative mindset with cross\-functional teams across engineering, operations, and compliance.

and translate requirements into technical solutions. * Demonstrate personal ownership/responsibility for work with thorough follow through to conclusion along with flexibility and a quality focus. * Flexibility to deal with changing priorities as required. * Certifications in application delivery or modern software development and quality practices such as Agile, Product Management, and Quality Assurance are desired. * Acting professionally, ethically and with integrity. * Must have broad understanding of the full software development lifecycle * Self\-starter and willing to take initiative in problem identification and solutions provision using a systematic and organised approach to their work * Very Strong team player * Highly motivated **Desirable Requirements:** * IT qualification * A sound knowledge of financial services sector * Experience delivering to tight project timescales * ISEB qualification **What you will do:** * Defining the business problem, scope the business solution and help formulate business cases for IT steering; * Identifying options to address the business problem and help the business critically assess those options, including, understanding the impact of a proposed change on business processes, on customers and on a commercial basis. * Developing good working relationships with all stakeholders, including senior business stakeholders, SME’s, development teams, project managers and relevant 3rd party suppliers.

title: Test Automation Engineer
description: **The Company** Cubic³ provides advanced software\-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in\-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. The Role We are looking for an energetic and motivated **Test Automation Engineer** to join our DevOps and Quality Engineering team. This role will focus on **building, maintaining, and executing automated test solutions** that support our engineering teams. You will work closely with developers, QA engineers, and DevOps teams to help ensure high\-quality, scalable software delivery. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with solid automation experience who is looking to deepen their technical expertise and grow towards a senior or specialist role. Key Responsibilities * Design, develop, and maintain automated test suites across multiple systems and services * Contribute to the evolution of test automation frameworks and tooling * Execute automated and manual tests as required to validate features and releases * Investigate and troubleshoot test failures, identifying root causes and working with engineering teams on fixes * Collaborate with developers to ensure testability and quality are embedded early in the development lifecycle * Support integration of automated testing into CI/CD pipelines * Contribute to quality metrics and reporting, helping improve visibility of test outcomes * Ensure best practices in test automation are followed across the team * Participate in code reviews and continuous improvement initiatives **Experience \& Skills** * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related discipline * 3–5 years’ experience in software testing or test automation * Hands\-on experience with test automation tools and frameworks (e.g. Selenium, Cypress, Playwright or similar) * Experience working with programming languages including .Net/C\#/C\+\+ * Performance Testing experience, including Load and Stress testing * Understanding of API testing and test automation strategies * Experience working within Agile / Scrum environments * Exposure to CI/CD tools (e.g. Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) * Strong debugging and problem\-solving skills * Good communication and collaboration skills Nice to Have * Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) * Knowledge of containerisation (Docker, Kubernetes) * Exposure to performance or security testing tools * Familiarity with telecoms, IoT, or connected device ecosystems *New hires at* Cubic³ *are required to work onsite five days a week during their six\-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work\-from\-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles.* **Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.**

title: Treasury Analyst
description: **Treasury Analyst \- 11 month contract \- from €87,000 per annum pro rata \- 3 days hybrid in Dublin 4** Join a dynamic Cash Management \& Corporate Banking team and play a key role in supporting global treasury operations. This is a hands\-on opportunity to gain exposure to international finance, work across multiple systems, and contribute to a fast\-growing, high\-impact environment. **What You’ll Do** * Manage daily cash positions across multiple subsidiaries and bank accounts * Support domestic and international cash movements, including wire transfers * Assist with daily treasury payments and transaction processing * Maintain banking portals and user access controls * Support bank account administration (open/close accounts, KYC, documentation) * Confirm and settle foreign currency trades * Contribute to system testing for new implementations and upgrades * Help maintain a strong controls environment, including audit coordination * Provide ongoing support across treasury systems and processes **What You Bring** * Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, or Accounting * 5\+ years' experience in inhouse treasury operations * Strong analytical skills with advanced Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, etc.) * Familiarity with treasury/banking platforms (e.g., CashPro, CitiDirect, HSBCnet, 360T, Finastra) is a plus **This opportunity is an 11 month contract, paying from €87,000 per annum pro rata, hybrid in Dublin 4\. title: Treasury Analyst
description: **Description** The Treasury Analyst will be an integral part of our Cash Management and Corporate banking team, supporting many aspects of our global Treasury functions. In this role, you will gain invaluable experience with visibility into Meta’s operations and business and have a meaningful impact within a growing Finance organization. Successful candidates for this team are well\-rounded top performers who can be key contributors in a high\-intensity growth environment. Attention to detail, organization, and a deep passion to learn, will also be key to your success. As a Company with international operations, you will have the opportunity to develop an understanding of the issues involved in managing a complex global organization and be involved in creating and implementing solutions to these challenges. **Responsibilities:** This position is responsible for, but is not limited to, the following: * Actively manage daily cash balances among dozens of key subsidiaries and bank accounts * Support execution and coordination of intercompany and external cash movement, including wire transfers * Support Treasury team on day to day wire payments. * Assist the Treasury team on system maintenance around administration of banking portal access * Support a robust controls environment with regular interactions among internal and external audit functions * Support system administration across various internal systems. * Support with the Bank account administration tasks including opening/closing accounts KYC and other administrative tasks. * Perform confirmation and settlement of foreign currency trades * Provide support for testing of systems for new rollouts. - **Qualifications:** * A minimum of a Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance or Accounting * Experience in domestic and international Treasury Operations * Exceptional analytical skills and strong Excel skills (pivot tables, vlookups, etc.) * Knowledge of banking and treasury systems such as CashPro, CitiDirect, HSBCnet, 360T, Finastra is a plus **Skills and Abilities:** * Must be a self\-starter, have own initiative, and possess strong prioritization and organization skills * Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment and can handle multiple projects simultaneously * Must have good verbal, written, and presentation skills to articulate ideas and plans clearly, accurately, and effectively with all levels of internal and external audiences * Must be a team player adept at facilitating an open exchange of ideas and know how to build win/win relationships * Ability to manage multiple tasks under strict deadlines with meticulous attention to detail

title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Veterinary Nurse Opportunity** **Shenick Veterinary Centre, Skerries, Co. Dublin** **Full\-time \| Permanent** **Salary: €32,000 – €40,000 DOE** **Start date: June/July 2026 (flexible)** **A Rare Opportunity to Join a Clinically Driven Team** Opportunities within this practice don’t come around often — making this a truly unique chance to join a highly respected, clinically focused veterinary team. Whether you’re a 2026 graduate ready to launch your career or an experienced RVN seeking a fresh, progressive environment, this role offers the perfect balance of support, autonomy, and professional growth. **The Role: Varied, Hands\-On \& Rewarding** This Is a Full\-time Position (40 Hours Across 5 Days) With a 1 In 3 Weekend Rota, Offering Excellent Exposure Across All Aspects Of Small Animal Nursing. You’ll Be Actively Involved In * Surgical and theatre nursing * Medical and clinical care * Inpatient monitoring and recovery * Dentistry and radiography * Laboratory diagnostics * Infection control and dispensary management * Client communication and education You’ll be trusted with genuine responsibility, while always supported by an experienced and collaborative team. Whether you're building confidence or expanding your expertise, you’ll be encouraged to develop at your own pace and truly feel part of the practice. **Working Hours** * Monday–Friday: 8:30am – 6:30pm * Saturday: 9:30am – 4:00pm * No out\-of\-hours, overnight, or Sunday shifts Enjoy a role that supports both clinical excellence and a healthy work\-life balance. **About The Practice** Shenick Veterinary Hospital is a modern, purpose\-built facility designed to support high standards of clinical care. The practice is fully equipped with digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound, an in\-house laboratory, as well as laser therapy and hydrotherapy facilities. The hospital itself is spacious and thoughtfully laid out, with separate wards for cats and dogs, a large prep and procedure area, two operating theatres, an isolation ward, and multiple consultation rooms. Staff facilities are equally well considered, with comfortable break areas and ample workspace, creating an environment that is both professional and enjoyable to work in. Established in 2014, the Skerries practice serves the wider communities of Skerries, Rush, and Lusk. You’ll be joining a close\-knit and collaborative team of vets, nurses, and support staff who are genuinely committed to delivering high\-quality patient care. **A Strong Clinical Network** As part of a wider group that includes sister clinics in Balbriggan and Stamullen, the practice offers access to a wider clinical network of experienced veterinary professionals, including certificate holders. This means opportunities for internal referrals, collaboration on cases, and ongoing clinical mentorship are always available, helping you continue to grow and develop throughout your career. **About You** We are looking for a veterinary nurse who takes pride in their work and enjoys being part of a supportive team environment. You will either be registered with the Veterinary Council of Ireland or due to qualify in 2026 and eligible to register. A positive attitude, strong communication skills, and a genuine passion for patient care are essential. You should be comfortable taking initiative, open to learning, and eager to contribute to a team that values collaboration and clinical excellence. This role is open to all experience levels, from newly qualified nurses looking to build confidence to experienced RVNs seeking a fresh and rewarding challenge. **Why Join Us?** We Believe That Investing In Our People Is The Key To Great Patient Care. Here’s What You Can Expect When You Join Our Team **Work Life Balance \& Wellbeing** * 22 days annual leave (pro\-rata) * Birthday leave * Paid maternity, paternity, and adoptive leave packages * Paid bereavement and sick leave * Employee Assistance Programme \& wellbeing centre access * Cycle to Work scheme * No OOH or Sunday shifts **Career Development** * €600 annual CPD allowance \+ 3 paid CPD days (pro\-rata) * Funding for Certificates, Diplomas, or even PhDs * Bespoke Nurse Evolve \& Graduate Nurse Academy programmes * Leadership Academy for aspiring Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Clear progression opportunities across the group **Professional Memberships \& Financial Benefits** * VCI membership and VDS cover paid * Employee pension contributions * Annual salary reviews * Discounted staff pet care across the entire IVC Evidensia network * Retail and wellbeing discounts via our “Pawesome Benefits” platform * Free flu jabs **Interested? Let’s Talk** If you’re looking for a role where you can grow, feel supported, and make a meaningful clinical impact, we would love to hear from you. Apply today or get in touch for a confidential chat — and discover how Shenick Veterinary Group can support your next career step. €32,000 \- €40,000 a year €32,000\-€40,000 DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: ***Raheny Veterinary Hospital, North Dublin*** ***Full\-time \| 12\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract (Maternity Cover)*** ***Salary €32,000\-€35,000 DOE*** ***Start Date: September 2026 (flexible)*** **Looking for the Perfect First Step \- or a Fresh Opportunity?** Whether you’re just about to qualify in 2026 or already working as a Veterinary Nurse and looking for a change, this is a fantastic opportunity to join a welcoming, highly regarded hospital team in Raheny. This maternity cover role is about supporting an already strong team during an exciting period of growth and development. It’s particularly well\-suited to graduate or junior nurses looking for a supportive, confidence\-building environment, but we’re equally open to nurses with more experience who want to develop further within a busy hospital setting. **A Brilliant Place to Start \- and Grow** This role offers the kind of experience that helps you become a confident, capable nurse. You’ll be fully supported from day one, with structured onboarding, hands\-on training, and a team that genuinely enjoys helping each other succeed. Raheny has been recognised internally as one of the best teams in the group, known for its friendly atmosphere, strong collaboration, and positive culture. It’s an environment where you can ask questions, build your skills, and feel part of something. At the same time, we’re undergoing major renovations and investment into the hospital, making it an incredibly exciting time to join. You’ll be part of a practice that is evolving, improving, and continuing to raise standards of care. **A Varied and Rewarding Nursing Role** This is a hands\-on clinical role where no two days are the same. You’ll be involved in all aspects of nursing, from surgical support and anaesthesia monitoring through to inpatient care, diagnostics, and nurse consultations. The caseload is varied, with a good balance of medical and surgical work, allowing you to develop a broad and interesting skillset. There is a real focus on quality patient care and clinical work\-ups, rather than fast turnover, giving you the opportunity to fully engage in cases and see them through. You’ll work closely with vets and colleagues across the team, playing an active role in patient care and the overall client journey. **A Structured Role with Work\-Life Balance** The role is based on a 5\-day week, with a mix of early and late shifts (typically between 7:30am and 9pm), alongside a shared weekend rota of approximately 1 in 6 Saturdays. Importantly, there is no out\-of\-hours or on\-call requirement, allowing you to enjoy a healthy balance between your work and personal life. Flexibility is offered where possible, in line with the needs of the clinic. **A Supportive, Community\-Focused Hospital** Raheny Veterinary Hospital is a long\-established, community\-focused practice with strong, lasting relationships with its clients. The clinic is well\-equipped with modern facilities including digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound, endoscopy, in\-house laboratory equipment, and dedicated surgical and inpatient areas. You’ll be joining a large, experienced team of vets, nurses, animal care assistants, and support staff, all working closely together. The culture is built on kindness, teamwork, and mutual support, with a shared goal of delivering excellent patient care while enjoying the work we do. **About You** We’re looking for a Veterinary Nurse who enjoys working as part of a team and takes pride in patient care. You’ll be organised, proactive, and comfortable in a busy clinical environment, with strong communication skills and a positive attitude. This role is ideal for new graduates joining the register in 2026 who want a structured and supportive start to their careers. It also suits nurses with some experience who are keen to continue developing within a larger hospital environment. You’ll need to be eligible for VCI registration to be considered. **Benefits (Pro\-Rata)** We offer a competitive and well\-rounded benefits package, which is applied on a pro\-rata basis for this fixed\-term role. This includes 22 days annual leave plus birthday leave, paid maternity, paternity and adoptive leave schemes, cycle\-to\-work, and access to our employee wellbeing programme, including EAP services and wellbeing resources. We’re committed to your development, with a €600 CPD allowance and paid CPD days, access to our Graduate Nurse Academy and internal training programmes, and full support for further qualifications or certificates. title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal

This maternity cover role is about supporting an already strong team during an exciting period of growth and development. It’s particularly well\-suited to graduate or junior nurses looking for a supportive, confidence\-building environment, but we’re equally open to nurses with more experience who want to develop further within a busy hospital setting. **A Brilliant Place to Start – and Grow** ----------------------------------------- This role offers the kind of experience that helps you become a confident, capable nurse. You’ll be fully supported from day one, with structured onboarding, hands\-on training, and a team that genuinely enjoys helping each other succeed. Raheny has been recognised internally as one of the best teams in the group, known for its friendly atmosphere, strong collaboration, and positive culture. It’s an environment where you can ask questions, build your skills, and feel part of something. At the same time, we’re undergoing major renovations and investment into the hospital, making it an incredibly exciting time to join. You’ll be part of a practice that is evolving, improving, and continuing to raise standards of care. **A Varied and Rewarding Nursing Role** --------------------------------------- This is a hands\-on clinical role where no two days are the same. You’ll be involved in all aspects of nursing, from surgical support and anaesthesia monitoring through to inpatient care, diagnostics, and nurse consultations. The caseload is varied, with a good balance of medical and surgical work, allowing you to develop a broad and interesting skillset. There is a real focus on quality patient care and clinical work\-ups, rather than fast turnover, giving you the opportunity to fully engage in cases and see them through. You’ll work closely with vets and colleagues across the team, playing an active role in patient care and the overall client journey. **A Structured Role with Work\-Life Balance** --------------------------------------------- The role is based on a 5\-day week, with a mix of early and late shifts (typically between 7:30am and 9pm), alongside a shared weekend rota of approximately 1 in 6 Saturdays. Importantly, there is no out\-of\-hours or on\-call requirement, allowing you to enjoy a healthy balance between your work and personal life. Flexibility is offered where possible, in line with the needs of the clinic. **A Supportive, Community\-Focused Hospital** --------------------------------------------- Raheny Veterinary Hospital is a long\-established, community\-focused practice with strong, lasting relationships with its clients. The clinic is well\-equipped with modern facilities including digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound, endoscopy, in\-house laboratory equipment, and dedicated surgical and inpatient areas. You’ll be joining a large, experienced team of vets, nurses, animal care assistants, and support staff, all working closely together. The culture is built on kindness, teamwork, and mutual support, with a shared goal of delivering excellent patient care while enjoying the work we do. **About You** ------------- We’re looking for a Veterinary Nurse who enjoys working as part of a team and takes pride in patient care. You’ll be organised, proactive, and comfortable in a busy clinical environment, with strong communication skills and a positive attitude. This role is ideal for new graduates joining the register in 2026 who want a structured and supportive start to their careers. It also suits nurses with some experience who are keen to continue developing within a larger hospital environment. You’ll need to be eligible for VCI registration to be considered. **Benefits (Pro\-Rata)** ------------------------ We offer a competitive and well\-rounded benefits package, which is applied on a pro\-rata basis for this fixed\-term role. This includes 22 days annual leave plus birthday leave, paid maternity, paternity and adoptive leave schemes, cycle\-to\-work, and access to our employee wellbeing programme, including EAP services and wellbeing resources. We’re committed to your development, with a €600 CPD allowance and paid CPD days, access to our Graduate Nurse Academy and internal training programmes, and full support for further qualifications or certificates. Professional memberships (VCI and VDS) are covered, and there are clear opportunities for career progression. Additional benefits include annual pay reviews, pension contributions, staff discounts on veterinary fees, retail and lifestyle discounts through our benefits platform, and access to wider company initiatives and communities. **Join Us at an Exciting Time** ------------------------------- This is more than a maternity cover role – it’s an opportunity to build your confidence, develop your skills, and be part of a genuinely supportive team during a really exciting phase of growth and investment. title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Veterinary Nurse Opportunity** ***Shenick Veterinary Centre, Skerries, Co. Dublin*** ***Full\-time \| Permanent*** ***Salary: €32,000 \- €40,000 DOE*** ***Start date: June/July 2026 (flexible)*** **A Rare Opportunity to Join a Clinically Driven Team** Opportunities within this practice don’t come around often — making this a truly unique chance to join a highly respected, clinically focused veterinary team. Whether you’re a 2026 graduate ready to launch your career or an experienced RVN seeking a fresh, progressive environment, this role offers the perfect balance of support, autonomy, and professional growth. **The Role: Varied, Hands\-On \& Rewarding** This is a full\-time position (40 hours across 5 days) with a 1 in 3 weekend rota, offering excellent exposure across all aspects of small animal nursing. You’ll be actively involved in: * Surgical and theatre nursing * Medical and clinical care * Inpatient monitoring and recovery * Dentistry and radiography * Laboratory diagnostics * Infection control and dispensary management * Client communication and education You’ll be trusted with genuine responsibility, while always supported by an experienced and collaborative team. Whether you're building confidence or expanding your expertise, you’ll be encouraged to develop at your own pace and truly feel part of the practice. Working Hours: * Monday\-Friday: 8:30am \- 6:30pm * Saturday: 9:30am \- 4:00pm * No out\-of\-hours, overnight, or Sunday shifts Enjoy a role that supports both clinical excellence and a healthy work\-life balance. **About The Practice** Shenick Veterinary Hospital is a modern, purpose\-built facility designed to support high standards of clinical care. The practice is fully equipped with digital and dental X\-ray, ultrasound, an in\-house laboratory, as well as laser therapy and hydrotherapy facilities. The hospital itself is spacious and thoughtfully laid out, with separate wards for cats and dogs, a large prep and procedure area, two operating theatres, an isolation ward, and multiple consultation rooms. Staff facilities are equally well considered, with comfortable break areas and ample workspace, creating an environment that is both professional and enjoyable to work in. Established in 2014, the Skerries practice serves the wider communities of Skerries, Rush, and Lusk. You’ll be joining a close\-knit and collaborative team of vets, nurses, and support staff who are genuinely committed to delivering high\-quality patient care. **A Strong Clinical Network** As part of a wider group that includes sister clinics in Balbriggan and Stamullen, the practice offers access to a wider clinical network of experienced veterinary professionals, including certificate holders. This means opportunities for internal referrals, collaboration on cases, and ongoing clinical mentorship are always available, helping you continue to grow and develop throughout your career. **About You** We are looking for a veterinary nurse who takes pride in their work and enjoys being part of a supportive team environment. You will either be registered with the Veterinary Council of Ireland or due to qualify in 2026 and eligible to register. A positive attitude, strong communication skills, and a genuine passion for patient care are essential. You should be comfortable taking initiative, open to learning, and eager to contribute to a team that values collaboration and clinical excellence. This role is open to all experience levels, from newly qualified nurses looking to build confidence to experienced RVNs seeking a fresh and rewarding challenge. **Why Join Us?** We believe that investing in our people is the key to great patient care. Here’s what you can expect when you join our team: **Work Life Balance \& Wellbeing** * 22 days annual leave (pro\-rata) * Birthday leave * Paid maternity, paternity, and adoptive leave packages * Paid bereavement and sick leave * Employee Assistance Programme \& wellbeing centre access * Cycle to Work scheme * No OOH or Sunday shifts **Career Development** * €600 annual CPD allowance \+ 3 paid CPD days (pro\-rata) * Funding for Certificates, Diplomas, or even PhDs * Bespoke Nurse Evolve \& Graduate Nurse Academy programmes * Leadership Academy for aspiring Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Clear progression opportunities across the group **Professional Memberships \& Financial Benefits** * VCI membership and VDS cover paid * Employee pension contributions * Annual salary reviews * Discounted staff pet care across the entire IVC Evidensia network * Retail and wellbeing discounts via our “Pawesome Benefits” platform * Free flu jabs **Interested? Let’s Talk** If you’re looking for a role where you can grow, feel supported, and make a meaningful clinical impact, we would love to hear from you. Apply today or get in touch for a confidential chat — and discover how Shenick Veterinary Group can support your next career step. title: Virtual Analytics Network (VAN) PhD Graduate Program
description: **Summary** Novartis continues to accelerate its growth in the data and digital space. As part of our continued growth, we are hiring PhD Graduates into our Advanced Quantitative Sciences (AQS) community who will start their career with 15 months rotating across 3 key areas of the organization. You will have the opportunity to work in different disease areas and phases of drug development, and then at the end of your rotations you will choose the area in which you will work permanently! We are excited to offer this unique opportunity to join a group of innovative PhD graduates working within our Virtual Analytics Network in the UK or Ireland. This is a truly unique PhD Graduate Program! During your rotations, you will use your strong quantitative skills and curious mindset to provide expert support to clinical trials in early or late phase clinical development and / or exploratory analyses. Throughout, you will be supported with structured training and a buddy system, plus you will have the opportunity to meet and collaborate with colleagues in other locations. Once you have completed your rotations, in your permanent role as a Principal Biostatistician you will be responsible for all statistical work relating to one or more assigned trials in collaboration with the clinical trial team. You may also, with appropriate supervision, support project level goals and lead the implementation of modern and innovative clinical trial/experimental designs, statistical modelling, statistical analyses and data explorations at the study level. **About The Role** **Location:** UK (hybrid in our London offices or remote working) or Ireland **Duration:** 15 months **Start date:** flexible between September 2026 to latest February 2027 The program is set for 15 months, but you will be employed on a permanent contact from the start. **Important Information** * Applications are open until June 7th * Please note that we can only accept applicants who are eligible to work in the U.K. or Ireland * Please submit a cover letter that includes your motivation for the program and from when you would be available to start. Thank you. **Major Accountabilities** * All statistical tasks on assigned trials, where you will be expected to perform these tasks independently, seeking peer input/review as required. Tasks may include: protocol and statistical analysis plan development; reporting activities; contributing to the planning and execution of exploratory analyses and/or PK, PK/PD analyses; exploratory biomarker and diagnostic analyses; statistical consultation. You will be responsible for initiating, driving and implementing novel methods and innovative trial designs in alignment with the Lead Statistician. * Explaining statistical methodologies and interpreting analysis results. You will provide statistical expertise to support submission activities and documents, meetings with and responses to Health Authorities and other drug development activities. * Contributing to interactions with external review boards, ethics committees, external consultants, and other external parties with oversight. You may represent Novartis in statistical discussions at external congresses, conferences or scientific meetings. * Representing the Analytics line function in multi\-functional teams for the assigned trials. You will have responsibility for ensuring functional alignment and line function awareness of the status and issues related to the assigned trials. * Collaborating with other line functions. You will explain statistical concepts in a manner that is easily understood by non\-statisticians and provide adequate statistical justifications for actions/decisions/statements. * Establishing and maintaining sound working relationships and effective communication with the Clinical Trial Team and quantitative sciences team. * Overseeing all Biostatistics resources and work for assigned trials and/or related non\-clinical activities, and ensuring work is timely and of adequate quality. **Essential Requirements** * PhD (in Statistics, Mathematics, Computational Sciences, Data Science, or equivalent); PhD needs to be completed before your start date; you can have max. 2 years of work experience after completion of your PhD * Proven knowledge and expertise in statistics and its application to clinical trials or public health * Proficiency in statistical software packages (e.g. R) * Ability to influence decisions that directly impact the assigned clinical trial and team ability to deliver objectives * Good communication and presentation skills, ability to work on and collaborate seamlessly with a multidisciplinary team to achieve team objectives * Proficiency in English (oral \& written) **Desirable** * Depending on your assignment, you it may be helpful to have expertise in one or more of the following areas: Bayesian statistics, causal inference, clinical trial design, data exploration skills, estimands, evidence synthesis, pharmacokinetics, machine learning, missing data, modelling approaches. **Why Novartis:** Helping people with disease and their families takes more than innovative science. It takes a community of smart, passionate people like you. Collaborating, supporting and inspiring each other. Combining to achieve breakthroughs that change patients’ lives. Ready to create a brighter future together? https://www.novartis.com/about/strategy/people\-and\-culture **Benefits and Rewards:** Learn about all the ways we’ll help you thrive personally and professionally. Read our handbook (PDF 30 MB)

title: Workflow Administrator
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC are currently looking for an experienced **Workflow Administrator** to join our team. The successful Administrator will be supporting the delivery of projects across all aspects of the business from an administration, reporting and coordination point of view. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** * Assist in the development and refinement of forecasting models and methodologies. * Continuously evaluate and streamline reporting processes to improve efficiency and accuracy. * Create a forecast of future work volumes and identify optimum resource capacity required to undertake. * Programme works in accordance with operational capability, commercial targets, and client KPI's. * Liaise with Operations regarding scheduling issues. * Produce and distribute Management Information (Reports) and identify and act upon areas of poor Performance. * Carry out weekly "Plan Do" Reviews with Operations to assess the effectiveness of the Workflow function. * Ensure Crews are compliant with the Maximo/Click/Syclo process and challenge and manage individual performance issues. * Manage customer and client escalations. * Contribute towards the departments approach to continuous improvement and utilisation of information Technology. * Ability to process and submit payments and completions. * Ad hoc tasks as required by the wider business. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Previous experience within the construction or utilities sectors, desirable. * Previous experience submitting payments and completions, desirable. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex data sets. * Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey technical information to non\-technical stakeholders. * Detail\-oriented with a focus on accuracy and attention to detail. * Confident, motivated and can work independently as well as part of a team. * Ability to work under pressure with strict deadlines. * Time management abilities to meet targets with the ability to prioritise stakeholder impact. * Good communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. * Integrity and respect for confidentiality and privacy. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.
